cards.raw_source_text: Zuo yue zhi xin: At the start of each round, you gain +1 speed this round for each player's initiative that matches your experience.

cards.name: Dao

abilities.name: Tan xing ren
abilities.text: This weapon can attack any space in your basic attack grid at +0.

cards.flavor: Used in many styles of kung fu, the dao is often considered the root of short weapons in China.
cards.raw_source_text: Tan xing ren: This weapon can attack any space in your basic attack grid at +0.

cards.name: Hu Xion Jia

abilities.name: Gang tie zhi fu
abilities.text: Your defense rolls gain +2 until the next time you attack or use this action.

cards.flavor: Form-fitted to each user, the breastplate could deflect spear and arrow blows to the chest while remaining light enough to allow easy movement for attacking.
cards.raw_source_text: Gang tie zhi fu - Action: Your defense rolls gain +2 until the next time you attack or use this action.

cards.name: Ba Duan Jin

abilities.name: Jun shi xun lian
abilities.text: If your inspiration and warrior are the same element, you may attack with a weapon an additional time each round.

cards.flavor: The Eight-Section Brocade is an ideal lifetime exercise and was used by the troops of Yue Fie to condition themselves for battle.
cards.raw_source_text: Jun shi xun lian: If your inspiration and warrior are the same element, you may attack with a weapon an additional time each round.

cards.name: Cleopatra VII

abilities.name: 'fd
abilities.text: At the start of each round, if there is an opposing male warrior, you may move one space.

cards.flavor: Born into a crumbling Egypt slowly losing ground to the expanding Roman Empire, Cleopatra used every tool at her disposal to maintain independence and return her nation to the glory that it once held.
cards.background:
Queen of a crumbling Egypt at only 18, Cleopatra used her wits and intelligence to pit the might of Rome against itself for over twenty years. She charmed both Julius Caesar and Marcus Antonius, outlived 3 sisters and a brother, ruled as the last pharaoh of Egypt and proclaimed herself the New Isis, Queen of Kings.

With Ast, goddess of death and rebirth watching over her, Cleopatra brings her cunning and wiles into the arena. With her power mandated in the Heqa and Was Scepters she bears, Cleopatra brings a new approach to combat in Anachronism™.
cards.raw_source_text: 'fd: At the start of each round, if there is an opposing male warrior, you may move one space.

cards.name: Ast

abilities.name: Nfr
abilities.text: Opposing cards cannot discard your support cards.

abilities.name: Xwi
abilities.text: After you hit an opponent, you may discard this card to gain 1 life and deal 1 damage to the defending warrior.

cards.flavor: By tricking Ra, the great mother-goddess Ast was able to gain his powers. She was a healer and the great magician of the gods.
cards.raw_source_text:
Nfr: Opposing cards cannot discard your support cards.
Xwi: After you hit an opponent, you may discard this card to gain 1 life and deal 1 damage to the defending warrior.

cards.name: B'gsw

abilities.name: Sft m s'
abilities.text: This weapon deals +2 damage while you are in a space directly behind or diagonally behind your opponent. You may use another weapon in this game.

cards.flavor: Easily concealed in the linen and silk robes of Cleopatra's time, the dagger was a deadly close-ranged weapon, capable of striking with very little warning.
cards.raw_source_text: Sft m s': This weapon deals +2 damage while you are in a space directly behind or diagonally behind your opponent. You may use another weapon in this game.

cards.name: Golden Corselet

abilities.name: Nw
abilities.text: Your defense rolls gain +3. If you are hit, discard this card.

cards.flavor: After befriending Julius Caesar, Cleopatra was taken to Rome. She stayed at the house of Caesar and took an active part in the social politics of Rome until he was murdered on the steps of the senate.
cards.raw_source_text: Nw: Your defense rolls gain +3. If you are hit, discard this card.

cards.name: Heqa and Was Scepters

abilities.name: Xq't inqt
abilities.text: Before you make an attack roll, you may discard this card. If you do, and the attack hits, the attack is a critical attack.

cards.flavor: Scepters offered and represent power and protection. They also set the king apart from everyone else and conveyed his authority, both secular and religious.
cards.raw_source_text: Xq't inqt: Before you make an attack roll, you may discard this card. If you do, and the attack hits, the attack is a critical attack.

cards.name: Horemheb the Restorer

abilities.name: Pr"
abilities.text: For each Aether card in play, your attack and defense rolls gain +1 and your weapon attacks deal +1 damage.

cards.flavor: Ascending to become pharaoh after Tutankhamun's death, Horemheb returned the old gods to their rightful place of worship.
cards.background:
Rising in status from a lowly officer in the Egyptian army, Horemheb served as the Great Commander of the Army under Akhenaten and Tutankhamun before becoming the last pharaoh of the 18th Dynasty. His conquests against the Hittites in Syria gave rise to his fame and legend and he ascended to the throne of Egypt through his skill and ambition, with not a drop of royal blood in his veins.

Wielding the wicked Khopesh sword, Horemheb gives praise to Horus, the god of kings on earth. In return, Horus has gifted him with an Udjat Eye, giving Horemheb great vitality and endurance. With divine protection fused to mortal ambition, who can oppose Horemheb in the Anachronism™ arena?
cards.raw_source_text: Pr": For each Aether card in play, your attack and defense rolls gain +1 and your weapon attacks deal +1 damage.

cards.name: Horus

abilities.name: Ib 'nch
abilities.text: Once per roll, after the dice are rolled, you may deal one damage to your warrior. If you do, force any one die to be re-rolled (limit once per roll).

cards.flavor: Son of Ast (or Isis) and Osiris, Horus fought against his uncle Seth for control of Egypt. Eventually, he won and exiled Seth to the Sahara Desert, but not before his eye was destroyed by Seth.
cards.raw_source_text: Ib 'nch: Once per roll, after the dice are rolled, you may deal one damage to your warrior. If you do, force any one die to be re-rolled (limit once per roll).

cards.name: Khopesh

abilities.name: Hd 's
abilities.text: If the defender has a polearm, your attack rolls with this weapon gain +2 and this weapon deals +1 damage.

cards.flavor: Edged on the inside or outside curve, or both, the khopesh was a significant development in sword technology. Edged on the outside, the weapon was effective at creating slashing wounds. Edged on the inside, it was used for hacking an opponent.
cards.raw_source_text: Hd 's: If the defender has a polearm, your attack rolls with this weapon gain +2 and this weapon deals +1 damage.

cards.name: Man-High Shield

abilities.name: Wch'
abilities.text: Your defense rolls gain +1 against one handed weapons.

abilities.name: Ikr
abilities.text: At the start of any round you may discard this card. If you do, your defense rolls get -1 and you gain +1 speed for this round.

cards.flavor: With the bow dominating tactical considerations on the Egyptian battlefield, the extreme size and bulk of these shields was borne out of nesessity.
cards.raw_source_text:
Wch': Your defense rolls gain +1 against one handed weapons.
Ikr: At the start of any round you may discard this card. If you do, your defense rolls get -1 and you gain +1 speed for this round.

cards.name: Udjat Eye

abilities.name: Xk'
abilities.text: Each time you roll a 1 on any die, gain 1 life.

cards.flavor: The eye of the god Hurus, Udjat means "whole" or "undamaged" and was reputed to have great healing and protective power.
cards.raw_source_text: Xk': Each time you roll a 1 on any die, gain 1 life.

cards.name: Ramses II

abilities.name: Xdb
abilities.text: If you have not made an attack this turn, make an attack with double the bonus from the appropriate attack grid. Your other attack rolls this round may not be increased by abilities or attack grids.

cards.flavor: Ramses II the Great ruled Egypt for over 60 years, building temples all along the Nile and creating statues that have stood for thousands of years.
cards.background: N/A Starter Warrior
cards.raw_source_text: Xdb - Action: If you have not made an attack this turn, make an attack with double the bonus from the appropriate attack grid. Your other attack rolls this round may not be increased by abilities or attack grids.

cards.name: Ra

abilities.name: Ri'
abilities.text: At the start of each round, if you have less life than all opponents and a special card in play, gain 1 life and deal 1 damage to an opposing warrior. You may use another inspiration this game.

cards.flavor: Born every morning, dying every night, Ra the sun god traveled the heavens each day, fighting his great enemy Apep, the snake.
cards.raw_source_text: Ri': At the start of each round, if you have less life than all opponents and a special card in play, gain 1 life and deal 1 damage to an opposing warrior. You may use another inspiration this game.

cards.name: Minb

abilities.name: Mnmn
abilities.text: If you miss an attack with this weapon, the next hit you make with this weapon deals +1 damage.

cards.flavor: Infantry armed with battle axes were typically deployed after the enemy had been weakened by archers. The axe was more effective in cutting wounded or fleeing enemies to pieces than it was in breaking an intact battle line.
cards.raw_source_text: Mnmn: If you miss an attack with this weapon, the next hit you make with this weapon deals +1 damage.

cards.name: Ikm''T

abilities.name: ' r hnn
abilities.text: When you are hit, the next support card you reveal is gains +3 initiative.

abilities.name: Mn
abilities.text: At the start of each round, if you won initiative, your defense rolls this round gain +2.

cards.flavor: Used sparingly and only by those who could afford its great cost, scale armor was worn by attackers in chariots to protect their torso from projectile weapons.
cards.raw_source_text:
' r hnn: When you are hit, the next support card you reveal is gains +3 initiative.
Mn: At the start of each round, if you won initiative, your defense rolls this round gain +2.

cards.name: Wereryet

abilities.name: Hdhd chbn
abilities.text: At the start of the round, if you won initiative, you may move one square.

cards.flavor: Used by the Egyptian military for hundreds of years, the wereryet chariot could travel the desert with great speed and it provided an excellent platform from which to launch spears and arrows.
cards.raw_source_text: Hdhd chbn: At the start of the round, if you won initiative, you may move one square.

cards.name: Seti I

abilities.name: Mnch n
abilities.text: You may have two inspirations in play. While you do, your basic attacks deal +1 damage.

cards.flavor: Seti I waged aggressive campaigns against the Hittites and Libyans, reclaiming much of Egypt's prestige abroad. He was eventually succeeded by his son, Ramses II.
cards.background:
Seti I brought stability to a devastated Egypt through conquest and construction. After the failures of the Amarna kings before him, Seti rebuilt Egypt's empire. His far-sighted goals set the foundation for all of the future Egyptian pharaohs and brought prosperity to his people.

Wearing the Khepresh war crown, bearing the the Egyptian Book of the Dead and guarded by Osiris, god of the underworld, Seti enters the Anachronism™ arena without fear of death, ready to conquer all who stand before him.
cards.raw_source_text: Mnch n: You may have two inspirations in play. While you do, your basic attacks deal +1 damage.

cards.name: Osiris

abilities.name: S'nch
abilities.text: When your are reduced to less than 1 life, instead of defeating your warrior, discard this card to set your life to 1.

cards.flavor: The god of death and the underworld, Osiris also presided over growth and vegetation. He was married to Ast (or Isis). Osiris was killed by his brother, Seth, who tricked him into a coffin before casting it into the Nile river.
cards.raw_source_text: S'nch: When your are reduced to less than 1 life, instead of defeating your warrior, discard this card to set your life to 1.

cards.name: Hornbow

abilities.name: Nis
abilities.text: You may discard a face-up non-ranged weapon you own to put this card into play from your discard pile.

abilities.name: Shw
abilities.text: This weapon deals +1 damage if you have not moved this turn.

cards.flavor: Made by joining two pieces of antelope horn with a center piece of wood, the earliest horn bows fired arrows tipped with flint or hardwood and fletched with three feathers.
cards.raw_source_text:
Nis - Action: You may discard a face-up non-ranged weapon you own to put this card into play from your discard pile.
Shw: This weapon deals +1 damage if you have not moved this turn.

cards.name: Khepresh

abilities.name: Xq't m"t
abilities.text: Opposing warriors may not use abilities on inspiration cards. An opposing warrior may spend an action to discard this card. Your cards cannot prevent this card from being discarded.

cards.flavor: This ceremonial bright crown made of cloth and adorned with golden disks was known as the blue or war-crown and was easily recognizable, even during battle.
cards.raw_source_text: Xq't m"t: Opposing warriors may not use abilities on inspiration cards. An opposing warrior may spend an action to discard this card. Your cards cannot prevent this card from being discarded.

cards.name: Reu Nu Pert Em Hru

abilities.name: Bswt
abilities.text: Warriors cannot gain life.

abilities.name: Snb
abilities.text: All other warriors with more than their printed life are reduced to their printed life.

cards.flavor: The Egyptian Book of the Dead was written on papyrus. It was a collection of mystical spells and formulae that ensured an Egyptians' passage into the afterlife.
cards.raw_source_text:
Bswt: Warriors cannot gain life.
Snb - Reveal: All other warriors with more than their printed life are reduced to their printed life.

cards.name: Srqt. The Scorpion King

abilities.name: Sh'w r wrt
abilities.text: Once per game, after you make a roll, you may change one of the dice to a six.

cards.flavor: Legendary even to the pharaohs of the old kingdoms, Srqt conquered all of Egypt centuries before the great dynasties
cards.background:
Nearly legendary even to the people of the Old Kingdom, Srqt ruled all of Egypt before the dynasties of the pharaohs. He led his armies to conquer all of the surrounding nations and his subjects lived in a time of prosperity and wealth.

Wielding spear and mace, Srqt brings his legacy of power and skill to the Anachronism™ arena. He is once again ready for battle, this time to face off against the upstart warriors who have followed in his wake and emerge victorious.
cards.raw_source_text: Sh'w r wrt: Once per game, after you make a roll, you may change one of the dice to a six.

cards.name: Khnum

abilities.name: M' 'n
abilities.text: Your next attack deals +1 damage.

abilities.name: ' r hnn
abilities.text: Your attack rolls gain +2 against warriors without a face-up inspiration.

cards.flavor: The progenitor god of Egypt, he created all of the gods and the lands. He ruled the waters, both of the rivers and of the sea.
cards.raw_source_text:
M' 'n - Reveal: Your next attack deals +1 damage.
' r hnn: Your attack rolls gain +2 against warriors without a face-up inspiration.

cards.name: Nsywt

abilities.name: Qm'
abilities.text: When you are hit by a non-adjacent opponent, deal 1 damage to the attacker. You may use another weapon.

cards.flavor: One of the earliest types of weapon invented by man, the nsywt, a javelin, was little more than a sharpened stick in its earliest incarnation.
cards.raw_source_text:
Qm': When you are hit by a non-adjacent opponent, deal 1 damage to the attacker.
You may use another weapon.

cards.name: Ikm Mskw

abilities.name: Ysh'
abilities.text: Your defense rolls gain +1 while you have more life than the attacking warrior.

abilities.name: Dnd
abilities.text: At the start any round, you may discard this card deal 1 damage to your warrior. If you do, your attacks this round deal +1 damage.

cards.flavor: Made from thick antelope hide and held together with sinew and bone, this seemingly makeshift armor offered protection from early piercing weapons.
cards.raw_source_text:
Ysh': Your defense rolls gain +1 while you have more life than the attacking warrior.
Dnd: At the start any round, you may discard this card deal 1 damage to your warrior. If you do, your attacks this round deal +1 damage.

cards.name: Mace of the Scorpion King

abilities.name: Sncht nbt
abilities.text: When you make an attack roll, if any of the dice are a 6, the attack deals +1 damage.

cards.flavor: This mace was created for ceremonial duties and to record the actions of Srqt. It told the stories of this conquests and of his patronage, as king, of agricultural development.
cards.raw_source_text: Sncht nbt: When you make an attack roll, if any of the dice are a 6, the attack deals +1 damage.

cards.name: Attila the Hun

abilities.name: Noyarqual
abilities.text: After any player attacks, your attack rolls this round gain +1 to a maximum of +6.

cards.flavor: Merciless leader of the Huns against the Romans, Visigoths and all who stood before them, Attila was known as the Scourge of God.
cards.background:
Attila the Hun began his reign over western Asia and eastern Europe in the fourth century AD. Drawn from Mongol stock, the Huns were forced westward by failing crops. Ruthless and without mercy, Attila saw to the devastation of many Gallic and Balkan cities. The “Scourge of God”, as he was known by a terrified Christian populace, led one of the most heavily armed forces of the era and used it with brutal and uncompromising efficiency.

Attila the Hun bears the Sword of Mars, which is said to make its wielder undefeatable on the field of battle. Driven on by the raging god Nemere, no opponent can hope to face Attila in the Anachronism™ arena without knowing the true meaning of fear.
cards.raw_source_text: Noyarqual: After any player attacks, your attack rolls this round gain +1 to a maximum of +6.

cards.name: Nemere

abilities.name: Qaugiqu salkin
abilities.text: At the start of each round, choose a direction. At the start of each player's turn, move their warrior one space in the chosen direction if possible.

cards.flavor: Nemere is the Hunnish god of war and the raging southern wind.
cards.raw_source_text: Qaugiqu salkin: At the start of each round, choose a direction. At the start of each player's turn, move their warrior one space in the chosen direction if possible.

cards.name: Sword of Mars

abilities.name: Amin-tu kurku qoorlal
abilities.text: If you have less than 3 life and hit your opponent with this weapon, that opponent is reduced to one life after the attack.

cards.flavor: Found by Attila on a forgotten battlefield, he claimed it to be the invincible sword of Mars, Roman god of war, and took it for himself.
cards.raw_source_text: Amin-tu kurku qoorlal: If you have less than 3 life and hit your opponent with this weapon, that opponent is reduced to one life after the attack.

cards.name: Duugla

abilities.name: Mon cinar-un tusqual
abilities.text: Once per round, after making a defense roll, you may set the roll equal to the attacking warrior's initiative for this round.

cards.flavor: Worn for centuries among the nomadic barbarians of central and western Asia, helmets of this type protected their wearers against the depredations of both battle and climate.
cards.raw_source_text: Mon cinar-un tusqual: Once per round, after making a defense roll, you may set the roll equal to the attacking warrior's initiative for this round.

cards.name: Gäitäy Geprech

abilities.name: Emiyel
abilities.text: When an opponent's attacks you and misses, they take 1 damage if their experience is less than 2. Otherwise, they get experience -2 for the rest of the game.

cards.flavor: In the reign of Attila, the Huns became the terror of the world, slaughtering cities and destroying civilizations.
cards.raw_source_text: Emiyel: When an opponent's attacks you and misses, they take 1 damage if their experience is less than 2. Otherwise, they get experience -2 for the rest of the game.

cards.name: Gengis Khan

abilities.name: Erkesiyeku ary-a
abilities.text: Your attack rolls are +1 while attacking an opponent without a cavalry card.

cards.flavor: Genghis Khan, the inspired leader of a tribe of steppe nomads, led his people to establish the Mongol Empire, the largest land empire in the history of mankind.
cards.background: N/A Starter Warrior
cards.raw_source_text: Erkesiyeku ary-a: Your attack rolls are +1 while attacking an opponent without a cavalry card.

cards.name: Koekki Moengke Tengri

abilities.name: Eguride-yin koke tngri
abilities.text: After you make an attack roll, you may discard this card to set the attack roll equal to your experience.

cards.flavor: Eternal Blue Heaven is the foremost of the spirits of heaven.
cards.raw_source_text: Eguride-yin koke tngri: After you make an attack roll, you may discard this card to set the attack roll equal to your experience.

cards.name: Alman Sukh

abilities.name: Cabciqu
abilities.text: Attacks with this weapon deal +1 damage when attacking an opponent without a face-up cavalry card.

cards.flavor: Each mongol warrior carried a battle axe (alman sukh), a curved sword (tomor schimitar), a lance (zhad), and two recurved bows (num mor and num tom).
cards.raw_source_text: Cabciqu: Attacks with this weapon deal +1 damage when attacking an opponent without a face-up cavalry card.

cards.name: Khurim

abilities.name: Kuiten-I sergeileku
abilities.text: Your opponent has no attack bonuses for this round.

cards.flavor: The design of the khurim jacket has been virtually unchanged for over a thousand years. It is traditionally worn by horsemen to protect them from the fierce Mongolian winters.
cards.raw_source_text: Kuiten-I sergeileku - Reveal: Your opponent has no attack bonuses for this round.

cards.name: Zhad

abilities.name: Am onggereku
abilities.text: Once per round, you may roll 2 dice. If the total is greater than an adjacent warrior's initiative for this round, deal 2 damage to that warrior or 3 damage if you have a face-up cavalry card. You may use another special card in this game.

cards.flavor: After using num mor and num tom to soften the enemy ranks, the zhad was employed to break the formations of opposing foot soldiers.
cards.raw_source_text: Am onggereku - Action: Once per round, you may roll 2 dice. If the total is greater than an adjacent warrior's initiative for this round, deal 2 damage to that warrior or 3 damage if you have a face-up cavalry card. You may use another special card in this game.

cards.name: Khutulun

abilities.name: Itegel
abilities.text: After you are missed by a basic attack, gain 1 life.

cards.flavor: The niece of Kublai Khan, Khutulun never gave her hand in marriage, insisting that each suitor best her in a wrestling match to win her affections.
cards.background:
The niece of Kublai Khan, Khutulun promised to marry any man that could best her in a wrestling challenge. Each challenger had to make a bet of one hundred horses on the outcome of the match. If the challenger won, Khutulun would marry him. If not, the horses were hers to keep. She was never beaten, remained unmarried and amassed over ten thousand horses from hapless suitors. She didn’t think it too many.

Khutulun’s gutal boots root her to the earth, while the holy mountain, Burqan-Qaldun, is a metaphor for her unconquerable spirit. Whether or not Khutulun’s opponent in the Anachronism™ arena intends to marry her, they had better bring one hundred horses with them and had better expect to lose them.
cards.raw_source_text: Itegel: After you are missed by a basic attack, gain 1 life.

cards.name: Burqan-Qaldun

abilities.name: Tuil-un qurduca
abilities.text: At the start of each round, all warriors whose initiative values for this round are odd gain +1 speed for this round.

cards.flavor: The sacred mountain has seen the launch of epic invasions that have conquered nations from one end of Asia to the other.
cards.raw_source_text: Tuil-un qurduca: At the start of each round, all warriors whose initiative values for this round are odd gain +1 speed for this round.

cards.name: Khutga

abilities.name: Konggen boloqu
abilities.text: You may use another weapon in this game.

abilities.name: Amua ezex
abilities.text: Discard this card and gain 2 life.

cards.flavor: Although mainly used for personal defense, the khutga played an important role in the cermonial lives of the Mongols.
cards.raw_source_text:
Konggen boloqu: You may use another weapon in this game.
Amua ezex - Action: Discard this card and gain 2 life.

cards.name: Gutal

abilities.name: Kodelgegentu cinar
abilities.text: After you gain life from another card, you may move one space.

cards.flavor: Boots, as with all Mongol clothing, were heavily constructed to withstand long journies and arduous conditions.
cards.raw_source_text: Kodelgegentu cinar: After you gain life from another card, you may move one space.

cards.name: 100 Aduu Bootsoo

abilities.name: Uridcilan medegsen teguskel
abilities.text: If you are the first player to deal damage after this card is revealed, your basic attacks deal +1 damage for the rest of the game.

cards.flavor: Each man who chose to vie for the hand of Khutulum had to bet 100 steeds on the outcome of their wrestling match. No man ever walked away with a wife or his horses.
cards.raw_source_text: Uridcilan medegsen teguskel: If you are the first player to deal damage after this card is revealed, your basic attacks deal +1 damage for the rest of the game.

cards.name: Kubulai Khan

abilities.name: Jabsartabal labtai ilaydan-a
abilities.text: Your attack rolls gain +1 for each of the defender's support cards that are a different culture than the defender.

cards.flavor: Grandson of Genghis Khan, Kublai established the foundations of a grand empire in China and Mongolia. He hosted Marco Polo, who returned to Europe with amazing stories of the mysterious East.
cards.background:
Grandson of the great Temujin, Kublai ruled his great empire from his palaces in China. Kublai proved his military acumen and might against his brother and uncle before securing his kingdom, defeating the Sung Dynasty, and invading both Japan and Java.

During the course of his reign, Kublai defeated kingdoms, rebellions, and countless other enemies. His military genius now comes to the Anachronism™ arena, where he will prove to the world that the Mongol Empire is alive and strong.
cards.raw_source_text: Jabsartabal labtai ilaydan-a: Your attack rolls gain +1 for each of the defender's support cards that are a different culture than the defender.

cards.name: Xanadu

abilities.name: Debajan
abilities.text: At the end of each round, if you dealt damage to another warrior, gain 1 life.

cards.flavor: During Kublai's reign, the summer capitol of the great Mongol empire was Xanadu, while the winter capitol was Dadu, which is modern-day Beijing.
cards.raw_source_text: Debajan: At the end of each round, if you dealt damage to another warrior, gain 1 life.

cards.name: Tomor Scimitar

abilities.name: Dutaydal-i ni baicayaqu
abilities.text: Once per game, before you make an attack roll, gain a damage bonus equal to your opponent's largest defense bonus +1.

cards.flavor: Curved to slice through men while riding past, the tomor scimitar was used by mounted warriors for man-to-man fighting once ranks broke.
cards.raw_source_text: Dutaydal-i ni baicayaqu: Once per game, before you make an attack roll, gain a damage bonus equal to your opponent's largest defense bonus +1.

cards.name: Tsamts Torgo

abilities.name: Oncoyoi qamayalalta
abilities.text: You may discard this card before an attack roll is made. If you do, the attack deals one less damage and the attacker may not use any card abilities for this attack.

cards.flavor: Originating in China, these undergarments made the rigors of distance riding in full battle gear bearable and offered unparalleled protection against arrow wounds.
cards.raw_source_text: Oncoyoi qamayalalta: You may discard this card before an attack roll is made. If you do, the attack deals one less damage and the attacker may not use any card abilities for this attack.

cards.name: Num Tom

abilities.name: Morgoldungen
abilities.text: You and a non-adjacent warrior each roll two dice. If your total is greater than thier experience, deal 1 damage to their warrior. If their total is less than your experience, deal 1 damage to their warrior.

cards.flavor: For use at great range, the num tom was the first weapon used in any Mongol cavalry attack, arcing arrows above the open land between the Mongols and their doomed opponents.
cards.raw_source_text: Morgoldungen - Reveal: You and a non-adjacent warrior each roll two dice. If your total is greater than thier experience, deal 1 damage to their warrior. If their total is less than your experience, deal 1 damage to their warrior.

cards.name: Subedei

abilities.name: Sain-tai tal-a-yi eriku
abilities.text: Once per round, when making an attack roll, you may re-roll one die of the attack roll. If the new result of the die is the same as the old result, deal one damage to the defending warrior.

cards.flavor: Genghis Khan's most brillian general, the renowned Subedei crushed the Kwarazmian Empire and moved on to subjugate the Russians and the Cumans.
cards.background:
Genghis Khan’s greatest general was responsible for more than sixty successful campaigns in Eastern Europe. A peerless general whose achievements stand out even against the likes of Alexander the Great, Subedei employed tactics and strategies that were decades ahead of his time. Without the brilliance of Subedei, Genghis Khan could never have established and ruled over the largest land empire the world has ever known.

Dedicated to the Yeke Mongghol Ulus, the great Mongol Empire, Subedei brings a different kind of combat to the Anachronism™ arena. Riding a mangudai steed, trained in the feigned flight tactic of the Mongols, Subedei’s opponents will quickly find themselves outmaneuvered and soundly defeated by this most cunning of warriors.
cards.raw_source_text: Sain-tai tal-a-yi eriku: Once per round, when making an attack roll, you may re-roll one die of the attack roll. If the new result of the die is the same as the old result, deal one damage to the defending warrior.

cards.name: Yeke Mongghol Ulus

abilities.name: Kucurkeg turimekei ulus
abilities.text: If you you have a face-up ranged weapon and you won initiative this round, move an opposing warrior one space.

cards.flavor: The Mongol Empire is the largest recorded land empire in the history of mankind. It stretched from the Pacific Ocean to the Black Sea and was created from a single family of Mongol nomads in just 25 years.
cards.raw_source_text: Kucurkeg turimekei ulus - Action: If you you have a face-up ranged weapon and you won initiative this round, move an opposing warrior one space.

cards.name: Num Mor

abilities.name: Qaralta-ban todolaqu
abilities.text: After an opponent moves for the second time in a turn, you may immediately make a basic attack against that warrior at +0.

cards.flavor: While harrying enemy ranks, the num mor could be used with speed and efficiency from horseback while the warrior used his legs to control his well-trained steed.
cards.raw_source_text: Qaralta-ban todolaqu: After an opponent moves for the second time in a turn, you may immediately make a basic attack against that warrior at +0.

cards.name: Khatangu

abilities.name: Caexu
abilities.text: Your defense rolls gain +1 if your experience is odd.

abilities.name: Tailqu
abilities.text: When you take damage from something other than an attack, you may discard this card to prevent the damage.

cards.flavor: A woven shirt covered with a layer of hardened leather plates, the khatangu was flexible to ease horseback riding while still providing protection from arrows and upward-directed spears.
cards.raw_source_text:
Caexu: Your defense rolls gain +1 if your experience is odd. 
Tailqu: When you take damage from something other than an attack, you may discard this card to prevent the damage.

cards.name: Mangudai Steed

abilities.name: Qayurmay-iyar bayuju ogku
abilities.text: Once per turn, after you move out of a space in an opposing warrior's attack grid, you may immediately attack that opponent with one of your ranged weapons at +0.

cards.flavor: The selection process for the elite Mangudai unit was grueling and dangerous. All of its members were superbly disciplined and trained, the perfect spearhead force for the attacking Mongol armies.
cards.raw_source_text: Qayurmay-iyar bayuju ogku: Once per turn, after you move out of a space in an opposing warrior's attack grid, you may immediately attack that opponent with one of your ranged weapons at +0.

cards.name: Charlemagne

abilities.name: Férocité inlassable
abilities.text: While you have at least three face-up support cards, your attack rolls gain +2.

cards.flavor: Victorious in over 60 campaigns and a pioneer of social reforms, Charlemagne laid the foundations for the Holy Roman Empire.
cards.background:
Personally leading his armies in 34 years of warfare and conquest, Charlemagne forged a mighty empire from the shattered remains of Western Europe. Crowned King of the Franks, Emperor of the Romans, and Protector of the Church, Charlemagne was devoted to his people, iron-fisted to his enemies, and devout in his faith. After ruling for nearly 50 years, Charlemagne’s empire stretched from Rome to the British Channel, and from Spain to Poland. Feared by his enemies and loved by the church and his people, he achieved unity and stability where none seemed possible.

Charlemagne brings tactical genius and a great, imposing presence to the Anachronism arena. His legendary exploits and great conquests decided the direction of the world and shaped the future of Europe in ways few others can match. Use Charlemagne, Imperator Romanum gubernans Imperium, to bring your enemies to their knees!
cards.raw_source_text: Férocité inlassable: While you have at least three face-up support cards, your attack rolls gain +2.

cards.name: Pape Hadrien Ier

abilities.name: Inspiration Divine
abilities.text: When you make a basic attack against a warrior with less experience, the attack roll gains +1. If the attack is a critical hit, it deals +1 damage.

cards.flavor: Rarely have the priesthood and the French Empire worked together so harmoniously and with such beneficial results to the Church and to humanity as during the lifetimes of Hadrien and Charlemagne.
cards.raw_source_text: Inspiration Divine: When you make a basic attack against a warrior with less experience, the attack roll gains +1. If the attack is a critical hit, it deals +1 damage.

cards.name: Épée Carolingienne

abilities.name: Lame Preste
abilities.text: While the defender has a two-handed weapon, attack rolls with this weapon gain +3.

cards.flavor: As sword-smithing technology advanced in the time of Charlemagne, swords became more tapered and the balance of weight shifted towards the hilt, facilitating faster swings and more precise swordplay.
cards.raw_source_text: Lame Preste: While the defender has a two-handed weapon, attack rolls with this weapon gain +3.

cards.name: Bouclier Carolingien

abilities.name: Prêt à Servir
abilities.text: While an opponent is in your basic attack grid, your defense and basic attack rolls gain +1. You may use another Armor.

cards.flavor: Because of their skill with disposable wood construction, Charlemagne ordered shield-makers stationed in every district and specifically brought them along on campaigns.
cards.raw_source_text: Prêt à Servir: While an opponent is in your basic attack grid, your defense and basic attack rolls gain +1. You may use another Armor.

cards.name: Couronne Impériale

abilities.name: Affirmation d'Autorité
abilities.text: Once per round, roll two dice. If the total is greater than your initiative for this round, gain 1 life. If the total is less than your experience, turn one of your support cards face up. At the end of the round, turn that card face down. If that card is discarded, use its initiative value as normal.

cards.flavor: "It is necessary that every man should seek to the best of his strength and ability to serve God and walk in the way of His precepts; for the Lord Emperor cannot watch over every man in personal discipline." -Article of Law
cards.raw_source_text: Affirmation d'Autorité - Action: Once per round, roll two dice. If the total is greater than your initiative for this round, gain 1 life. If the total is less than your experience, turn one of your support cards face up. At the end of the round, turn that card face down. If that card is discarded, use its initiative value as normal.

cards.name: Charles Martel

abilities.name: Inébranlable
abilities.text: After a roll has been made, you may discard a face-up support card you own to force any roll to be re-rolled. You may choose to give the re-roll +2 or -2.

cards.flavor: Imprisoned as a young man by his stepmother, Charles escaped and established himself as an unyielding leader and military commander, stopping the Saracen advance into Europe and strengthening the rule of the church in his kingdom.
cards.background:
Named Charles Martel, “The Hammer,” because of the merciless ways in which he smote the enemies of France, Martel spent his reign in an uninterrupted series of triumphant combats and conquests. He suppressed rebellious nobles in Friesland, defeated the Saracens in southern France, laid waste to the Saxon lands in Austrasia, demolished the armies of the Neustrians and Aquitainians, subdued all of Southern Germany, and massacred the attacking Saracen army led by Governor Abd~er~Rahman of Spain at the Battle of Poitiers.

Charles Martel brings brute force and straightforward tactics to the Anachronism arena. He is relentless in the pursuit of his enemies, fierce in battle, and unfailing in his devotion to victory. Use “The Hammer of France” to crush your enemies with decisive and uncompromising swiftness!
cards.raw_source_text: Inébranlable: After a roll has been made, you may discard a face-up support card you own to force any roll to be re-rolled. You may choose to give the re-roll +2 or -2.

cards.name: Saint Boniface

abilities.name: Conseil Avisé
abilities.text: If you win a tie or your opponent rolls doubles, your next attack deals +1 damage.

cards.flavor: Traveling with tireless energy and preaching fearlessly throughout all of France, Saint Boniface eventually met his end at the hands of an angry mob in Frisa.
cards.raw_source_text: Conseil Avisé: If you win a tie or your opponent rolls doubles, your next attack deals +1 damage.

cards.name: L'Épée aux Cinq Croix

abilities.name: Acier Sanctifié
abilities.text: Your defense rolls gain +1 for each inspiration the attacker and defender have in play.

cards.flavor: At the Battle of Poitiers, Charles Martel inspired his beleaguered and outnumbered army to stand up to the fearsome Saracen cavalry. Their courage and commitment won the day, and it is for this that Martel is most remembered.
cards.raw_source_text: Acier Sanctifié: Your defense rolls gain +1 for each inspiration the attacker and defender have in play.

cards.name: Broigne d'Écailles

abilities.name: Posture Défensive
abilities.text: Once per turn, after you make a defense roll, you may have the roll gain +3. If you do, you get -1 speed for your next turn.

cards.flavor: Only once in the history of armed combat has an army of infantry without bows and arrows or firearms withstood armored cavalry. This honor goes to the hauberk-armored men of Charles Martel and his brilliant defensive tactics.
cards.raw_source_text: Posture Défensive: Once per turn, after you make a defense roll, you may have the roll gain +3. If you do, you get -1 speed for your next turn.

cards.name: Marteau de Cavalerie

abilities.name: Coup Fracassant
abilities.text: At the start of each round, roll two dice. If the total is less than an adjacent opponent's experience, deal 1 damage to that warrior and their defense rolls get -1 this round.

cards.flavor: After witnessing the fearsome effectiveness of the Saracen cavalry, Charles Martel expanded on the traditional tactics of the time, and developed new and inventive ways to demonstrate his superiority on the battlefield.
cards.raw_source_text: Coup Fracassant: At the start of each round, roll two dice. If the total is less than an adjacent opponent's experience, deal 1 damage to that warrior and their defense rolls get -1 this round.

cards.name: Roland le Paladin

abilities.name: Foi jurée
abilities.text: At the start of each round, for each French card revealed that round, your attack rolls for that round gain +1 and you gain 1 life.

cards.flavor: Lord of the Breton March, the most celebrated of the Paladins of Charlemagne, Roland fought on when all seemed lost, holding true to his code of ideals even unto death.
cards.background:
With his legendary sword Durendal in hand, Roland, the Count of Mans, valiantly served his people in the service of his lord, Emperor Charlemagne. When ambushed by the Basques in the Roncevaux Pass, Roland’s adamant upholding of the Paladin code prevented him from calling for help. Though grievously wounded, he continued to battle with unwavering spirit and determination. He sacrificed his life in the name of his country, and in doing so immortalized himself in history as a symbol for the sanctity freedom and the preservation of belief.

Roland the Paladin brings dauntless courage and spotless virtue to the Anachronism arena. His enemies will fear his power and fortitude, and he will neither fail nor falter, no matter the sacrifices needed. Use the Count of Mans, the Knight of Blaives, to repulse your enemies’ charge and return the killing blow!
cards.raw_source_text: Foi jurée: At the start of each round, for each French card revealed that round, your attack rolls for that round gain +1 and you gain 1 life.

cards.name: Code du Paladin

abilities.name: Miséricorde
abilities.text: Your critical hits deal normal damage. Once per round, when you roll doubles on an attack roll, gain 1 life and immediately make an additional attack with your weapon if possible.

cards.flavor: Subject of the epic poem "La Chanson de Roland," Roland was said to have refused to call for help or run when faced with overwhelming odds, choosing instead to stand his ground and fight to his last breath.
cards.raw_source_text: Miséricorde: Your critical hits deal normal damage. Once per round, when you roll doubles on an attack roll, gain 1 life and immediately make an additional attack with your weapon if possible.

cards.name: Durendal

abilities.name: Regain de Courage
abilities.text: After you hit with this weapon, the next time you are dealt damage, reduce that damage by 1 to a minimum of 0.

cards.flavor: Sword of legend, said to have passed down through the ages from Hector himself, Roland brought its awesome might down upon all those who opposed him.
cards.raw_source_text: Regain de Courage: After you hit with this weapon, the next time you are dealt damage, reduce that damage by 1 to a minimum of 0.

cards.name: Cotte de Mailles du Paladin

abilities.name: Protection Renforcée
abilities.text: Before you are damaged by a critical hit, gain 3 life.

cards.flavor: When used correctly, chainmail could deflect otherwise mortal blows, allowing a professional soldier to survive the fury of battle. Its protection came, however, with a great deal of maintenance outside of battle.
cards.raw_source_text: Protection Renforcée: Before you are damaged by a critical hit, gain 3 life.

cards.name: Olifant

abilities.name: Appel à l'Aide
abilities.text: If you have less life than an adjacent opponent, discard this card to make three consecutive basic attacks against that opponent at +0.

cards.flavor: Fighting on until his last breath, Roland used his great hunting horn Olifant to summon the army of Charlemagne.
cards.raw_source_text: Appel à l'Aide - Action: If you have less life than an adjacent opponent, discard this card to make three consecutive basic attacks against that opponent at +0.

cards.name: Sainte Jeanne d'Arc

abilities.name: Sainte Colère
abilities.text: Your attack rolls gain +1 for each of your Armor cards in play. You may use up to three Armor cards this game. Your defense rolls can gain a bonus from only one of your cards.

cards.flavor: At only 17 years of age, she led the armies of France against the invading English, inspiring them with her piety, confidence, and faith.
cards.background: N/A Starter Warrior
cards.raw_source_text: Sainte Colère: Your attack rolls gain +1 for each of your Armor cards in play. You may use up to three Armor cards this game. Your defense rolls can gain a bonus from only one of your cards.

cards.name: Archange Michel

abilities.name: Présence Imposante
abilities.text: This round, if you are not an Earth or Water warrior, your basic attacks against warriors without an Armor card in play deal +1 damage.

cards.flavor: Claiming to have received visions from the Archangel Michael, Joan of Arc made her way from her family's village to Charles VII of France. Once there, she convinced him to give her supreme command of the French armies.
cards.raw_source_text: Présence Imposante - Reveal: This round, if you are not an Earth or Water warrior, your basic attacks against warriors without an Armor card in play deal +1 damage.

cards.name: Épée de Martel

abilities.name: Ardente Vengeance
abilities.text: After you are hit by an adjacent opponent's weapon, if you are a Fire warrior, you may immediately make an attack with this weapon at +0. This attack deals +1 damage.

cards.flavor: Retrieved from the Church of St. Catherine de Fierbois, Joan of Arc's sword was said to have been used by the great Charles Martel to defeat the Saracen army in the Battle of Poitiers.
cards.raw_source_text: Ardente Vengeance: After you are hit by an adjacent opponent's weapon, if you are a Fire warrior, you may immediately make an attack with this weapon at +0. This attack deals +1 damage.

cards.name: Blanc Harnois

abilities.name: Éclat Immaculé
abilities.text: Your attacks deal +1 damage to a player who has used a Reveal ability this game.

cards.flavor: After liberating Orleans, Jargeau, Meung-sur-Loire, Beaugency, and Patay from the English, Joan of Arc marched in her brilliant white armor to Rheims to see Charles crowned king.
cards.raw_source_text: Éclat Immaculé: Your attacks deal +1 damage to a player who has used a Reveal ability this game.

cards.name: Sainte Bannière

abilities.name: Bon Droit
abilities.text: After you are missed by an attack, your next successful attack deals +1 damage.

cards.flavor: "It had shared in the toil, it was just that it should share in the victory." -Attributed to Joan of Arc, said of her holy banner
cards.raw_source_text: Bon Droit: After you are missed by an attack, your next successful attack deals +1 damage.

cards.name: Vercingétorix

abilities.name: Vigueur Inépuisable
abilities.text: Defense rolls cannot be modified. Once per round when you hit, if the defender has no shield or Armor cards in play, your attack deals +1 damage.

cards.flavor: Vercingétorix ably unified the Gallic tribes, adopted the policy of retreating to natural fortifications, and undertook an early example of a scorched-earth strategy by burning towns to prevent the Roman legions from living off the land.
cards.background:
Unifying the fierce warriors of Gaul, Vercingétorix led the great Gallic revolt against the mighty Roman Empire. An inventive and resourceful leader, he was a military and political genius, assembling the tribes divided by the advancing army of Julius Caesar into a cohesive and organized force. His men continually harassed the advancing enemy armies, winning victory after victory while scorching the earth in the path of Romans, denying them shelter, food, and water. At the side of his comrades in arms, he personally led his raging army into battle, fighting for the freedom of his people and for their way of life.

Vercingétorix brings brutal ferocity, coupled with great intelligence, to the Anachronism arena. He continually exhausts his enemies, gaining ground wherever possible, expertly exploiting each and every weakness. Use this king among warriors to smash your enemies, one battle at a time!
cards.raw_source_text: Vigueur Inépuisable: Defense rolls cannot be modified. Once per round when you hit, if the defender has no shield or Armor cards in play, your attack deals +1 damage.

cards.name: Camulos

abilities.name: Rafale
abilities.text: This card's initiative value cannot be changed.

abilities.name: Attaque Soudaine
abilities.text: Make an immediate basic attack against an adjacent warrior at +0. Turn this card face down and swap it with another of your face-down cards. Turn the other card face up.

cards.flavor: Wielding an invisible sword, the vicious Celtic god of warfare and the sky was the inspiration for centuries of song, story, and warfare for his people.
cards.raw_source_text:
Rafale: This card's initiative value cannot be changed. 
Attaque Soudaine - Reveal: Make an immediate basic attack against an adjacent warrior at +0. Turn this card face down and swap it with another of your face-down cards. Turn the other card face up.

cards.name: Épée Celtique

abilities.name: Ravage
abilities.text: Your attack rolls with swords gain +1. You may use another sword. If you hit with two sword attacks in a single turn, gain +1 speed for that turn.

cards.flavor: Trained to use their surroundings to their advantage, the tribes of Gaul fought with stealth and guerrilla tactics, ambushing small groups of troops and retreating to safety when outnumbered.
cards.raw_source_text: Ravage: Your attack rolls with swords gain +1. You may use another sword. If you hit with two sword attacks in a single turn, gain +1 speed for that turn.

cards.name: Bouclier Celtique

abilities.name: Coup de Bouclier
abilities.text: Once per round, when you hit an opponent, if the highest die of the attack roll is greater than the highest die roll of the defense roll, the attack deals +1 damage and you may move the defender one space or rotate them.

cards.flavor: Meeting with little organized resistance, Caesar moved his armies through Gaul using a careful divide-and-conquer strategy. Vercingetorix successfully united many of the independent tribes to fight against the invading conqueror.
cards.raw_source_text: Coup de Bouclier: Once per round, when you hit an opponent, if the highest die of the attack roll is greater than the highest die roll of the defense roll, the attack deals +1 damage and you may move the defender one space or rotate them.

cards.name: Harnachement

abilities.name: Dégainer
abilities.text: Turn one of your face-down, one-handed weapon cards face up. At the end of the round, turn that card face down. If that card is discarded, use its initiative value as normal. You may use up to two weapons.

cards.flavor: Built to carry many different instruments of warfare, the Celtic war belt also served to protect the lower abdomen.
cards.raw_source_text: Dégainer - Reveal: Turn one of your face-down, one-handed weapon cards face up. At the end of the round, turn that card face down. If that card is discarded, use its initiative value as normal. You may use up to two weapons.

cards.name: Alarich

abilities.name: Zerstörer von Rom
abilities.text: After an ability on a Roman card is used, deal 1 damage to the warrior using the ability.

cards.flavor: After being crowned king of the Goths and proclaiming independence from the mighty Roman Empire, he proceeded to conquer vast swaths of territory throughout Europe, and ultimately sacked Rome herself.
cards.background:
After rising through the ranks in the Roman army, Alarich was made king of the Goths by the men in his ranks, who raised him up on a sheild to proclaim their independence from the might Roman Empire. In the years that followed, he marched on Constantinople, Athens, Sparta and Corinth, conquering vast swaths of territory throughout Europe. Using skills learned from his time in the Roman legions, he advanced to Italy and ultimately sacked Rome herself. She, who had been mistress of the world, now lay at the feet of the Goths.

Alarich brings calculated understanding of strategy, along with lessons leazrned from decades of warfare, to the anachronism arena. He strives to acheive great goals with relentless determination, never stopping or settling for less than his desires. Use this king of the Visigoths to bring your enemies to their knees!
cards.raw_source_text: Zerstörer von Rom: After an ability on a Roman card is used, deal 1 damage to the warrior using the ability.

cards.name: Heimdal

abilities.name: Wachsamkeit
abilities.text: You cannot be damaged by any opponent outside of that opponent's turn.

cards.flavor: As the watchman of the gods, Heimdal can see one hundred leagues in every direction and can even hear grass grow. He is the god of light, and guards Bifrost, the only entrance to Asgard. At the final conflict of Ragnarok, he will kill his age-old enemy, the evil god Loki.
cards.raw_source_text: Wachsamkeit: You cannot be damaged by any opponent outside of that opponent's turn.

cards.name: Ger

abilities.name: Aufgestaute Wut
abilities.text: After you hit with this weapon, if the attack roll was greater than the defender's experience, this weapon deals +1 damage for the rest of the game.

cards.flavor: Fighting with the Roman legions as foederati - foreign troops under Roman command - Alarich learned tactics that he eventually turned against the very empire that had taught him organized warfare.
cards.raw_source_text: Aufgestaute Wut: After you hit with this weapon, if the attack roll was greater than the defender's experience, this weapon deals +1 damage for the rest of the game.

cards.name: Wolfsfell

abilities.name: Wolfsschläue
abilities.text: At the start of each round, if your experience is less than each opponents' initiative for this round, you may move up to one space and your defense rolls this round gain +1.

cards.flavor: Able to deal with the harsh weather of northern European winters, local irregular troops were often used by the Roman Empire to suppress rebellions and uprisings in nearby provinces.
cards.raw_source_text: Wolfsschläue: At the start of each round, if your experience is less than each opponents' initiative for this round, you may move up to one space and your defense rolls this round gain +1.

cards.name: Salarische Tor

abilities.name: Großer Einmarsch
abilities.text: At the start of each round, if an opponent used a Reveal ability this round, your attack rolls gain +2 this round.

cards.flavor: With the death of Stilicho, the armies of the Roman Empire were left without a leader capable of stopping Alarich from advancing on Rome itself. Alarich pierced Rome's defenses at the northeast Salarian Gate and, with that, the city fell.
cards.raw_source_text: Großer Einmarsch: At the start of each round, if an opponent used a Reveal ability this round, your attack rolls gain +2 this round.

cards.name: Arminus

abilities.name: Waldscharmützel
abilities.text: After you take an action to move, your next attack and defense rolls gain +1.

cards.flavor: Returning from military training in Rome to his Cherusci homeland, he raised a great army to fight against the advancing Roman armies. In the Teutoburger Wald, he delivered to Rome her most crushing defeat, as three entire legions vanished overnight and were never heard from again.
cards.background:
Trained in Rome to command great armies, Arminius returned to his Cherusci homeland to raise a great host against the advancing Roman armies. In battle after battle, he remained one step ahead of the roman legions, always able to take advantage of their weaknesses, and alway able to ensure the most devastating defeats. He crushed forces led by Varus, Caecina, and Germanicus, staging ambushes and serving up such detailed and stunning defeats that the Roman perception and fear of the Germanic tribes were forever colored by his victories. His stalwart defense of the Germanic lands stopped the vast Roman Empire from ever conquering the lands east of the Rhine.

Arminius brings tactical genius in both feild warfare and guerrilla tactics to the anachronism arena. His swift, decisive actions catch opponents off guard, while his strength and stamina overpower and outlast all of his enemies. Use Arminius, the great Hermann der Cherusker, to ambush and decimate your enemies.
cards.raw_source_text: Waldscharmützel: After you take an action to move, your next attack and defense rolls gain +1.

cards.name: Tiwaz

abilities.name: Freier Himmel
abilities.text: After any player makes a critical hit, you may discard this card. If you do, the hit damages the attacker instead of the defender.

cards.flavor: God of war and the patron god of justice, Tiwaz is the oldest of the gods, inspiring courage and heroism in battle. He sacrificed his right hand to bind the wolf Fenrir, who, if set free, would devour the sun. At the day of Ragnarok, he will die from his battle wounds, but not before he slays the hound Garm, the guardian of hell.
cards.raw_source_text: Freier Himmel: After any player makes a critical hit, you may discard this card. If you do, the hit damages the attacker instead of the defender.

cards.name: Aaminius Standarte

abilities.name: Durchschlagende Massenandrang
abilities.text: Attacks with this weapon deal +2 damage if the defense roll is less than the defender's initiative.

cards.flavor: Using a wide array of weapons, the forces of Arminius crushed attacking Roman legions led by Varus, Caecina, and by Germanicus himself.
cards.raw_source_text: Durchschlagende Massenandrang: Attacks with this weapon deal +2 damage if the defense roll is less than the defender's initiative.

cards.name: Rüstung der Römisches Hilfstruppen

abilities.name: Leichtgewichtig
abilities.text: Your defense rolls gain +1. If you are missed by an attack and your defense roll is less than the attacker's experience, gain +1 speed on your next turn.

cards.flavor: Although foreign troops in the employ of Rome were not as well equipped as the Roman legionaries, when rebellion occurred, the effectiveness of even light Roman armor was made readily apparent.
cards.raw_source_text: Leichtgewichtig: Your defense rolls gain +1. If you are missed by an attack and your defense roll is less than the attacker's experience, gain +1 speed on your next turn.

cards.name: Varusschlacht

abilities.name: Waldlande
abilities.text: After your opponent uses a card ability to move their warrior, gain +1 speed on your next turn.

cards.flavor: Although promising to support Varus' advance into Teutoburger Wald, the Cherusci had no such intentions. The ghost of Varus' legions are still said to haunt the depths of the forest.
cards.raw_source_text: Waldlande: After your opponent uses a card ability to move their warrior, gain +1 speed on your next turn.

cards.name: Fredegunde

abilities.name: Betrügerischer Plan
abilities.text: You may make two attacks each turn with each of your knives. Your attack rolls gain +1 while you are directly or diagonally behind the defender.

cards.flavor: Rising from a lowly palace servant to become the Queen of Soissons, Fredegunde employed intrigue, manipulation, and assassination in order to secure her kingdom for herself and her son. In a murderous age, she proved that the shadows were the most dangerous weapon of all.
cards.background:
Treacherous consort of Chilperic I, Fredegunde rose from a lowly palace servant to become the Queen of Soissons. acheiving her goals through subtle manipulation and assassination, she was able to maintain both her position and the loyalty of her king. Exercising a great charismatic presence, she convinced her husband to have his previous wife strangled, to start a war with Austrassia, and to have Sigebert I - a rival - assissinated. In order to secure the throne for her own son, Chiperic's other heirs, followed by Chilperic himslef, met with tragic deaths during her reign. Unmatched in malicious plotting, this lowborn was one of the most influential people of her time.

Fredegunde brings assassination and vicious intrigue to the anachronism arena. Remorselessly climbing through society, Fredegunde never fails to bring allies under her influence with ensuring deaths of those who opposed her will. Use this deadly Queen of Soissons to subtly outmaneuver and overthrow your enemies.
cards.raw_source_text: Betrügerischer Plan: You may make two attacks each turn with each of your knives. Your attack rolls gain +1 while you are directly or diagonally behind the defender.

cards.name: Freya

abilities.name: Folkvang
abilities.text: After you make a roll, you may discard this card to set your roll equal to any player's initiative for that round.

cards.flavor: Goddess of fertility, war, and death, Freya flew between worlds with the aid of her feathered cloak and each day took half the souls of the warriors slain in battle with her to Folkvang.
cards.raw_source_text: Folkvang: After you make a roll, you may discard this card to set your roll equal to any player's initiative for that round.

cards.name: Vergifteter Dolch

abilities.name: Schleichendes Gift
abilities.text: At the start of each round, deal 1 damage to each warrior for each time they have been hit by this weapon this game.

cards.flavor: The chosen weapon of those warriors who strike from the shadows, with a few drops of poison, they can often accomplish more than skilled generals or powerful armies.
cards.raw_source_text: Schleichendes Gift: At the start of each round, deal 1 damage to each warrior for each time they have been hit by this weapon this game.

cards.name: Steppumhang

abilities.name: Behagliche Wärme
abilities.text: After you take damage, you may discard this card or get -1 speed on your next turn. If you do either, immediately move up to one space.

cards.flavor: Used for outdoor travel in harsh weather, quilted cloaks of this type were typically worn by both men and women to protect them from the elements and, where necessary, from prying eyes.
cards.raw_source_text: Behagliche Wärme: After you take damage, you may discard this card or get -1 speed on your next turn. If you do either, immediately move up to one space.

cards.name: Schutzherrin der Meuchelmörder

abilities.name: Sicherer Schlag
abilities.text: All modifiers in any of your attack grids that are less than +0 are increased to +2.

cards.flavor: An assassin's loyalty, such as it was, could be easily secured with the promise of gold.
cards.raw_source_text: Sicherer Schlag: All modifiers in any of your attack grids that are less than +0 are increased to +2.

cards.name: Siegfried

abilities.name: Solides Fundament
abilities.text: Your defense rolls that are less than 8 are increased to 8.

cards.flavor: Hero of  the Nibelungenlied and son of Sigmund, Siegfried bore the re-forged sword Balmung and the cursed ring Andvaranaut. He fought to win the hand of Krimhild, and  to help his friend win the hand of the warrior-queen Brunhild.
cards.background: N/A Promo Warrior
cards.raw_source_text: Solides Fundament: Your defense rolls that are less than 8 are increased to 8.

cards.name: Krimhild

abilities.name: Schildmaid
abilities.text: At the start of any round, you may give all of your attack rolls for that round -1. If you do, gain +2 experience and your defense rolls for that round gain +1.

cards.flavor: The beautiful daughter of King Dancrat, she married Siegfried, whom her brothers eventually killed. Vowing revenge, she eventually trapped them and had them beheaded.
cards.raw_source_text: Schildmaid: At the start of any round, you may give all of your attack rolls for that round -1. If you do, gain +2 experience and your defense rolls for that round gain +1.

cards.name: Balmung

abilities.name: Wild um sich schlagen
abilities.text: Once per round, your next attack this round with this weapon deals +2 damage.

cards.flavor: Balmung was said to have been forged by Wayland the Smith for Odin himself. Siegmund won the sword and passed it on to his son Siegfried, who re-forged it after Odin shattered it in battle.
cards.raw_source_text: Wild um sich schlagen - Action: Once per round, your next attack this round with this weapon deals +2 damage.

cards.name: Tarnkappe

abilities.name: Sich einfügen
abilities.text: Your defense rolls gain +2. If you move more than once in a turn or are hit, you loose this bonus until the start of the next round.

cards.flavor: Legend speaks of Siegfried's cloak Tarnkappe, the Cloak of Darkness, which he used to resuce and woo Brunhild for his brother-in-law Gunther.
cards.raw_source_text: Sich einfügen: Your defense rolls gain +2. If you move more than once in a turn or are hit, you loose this bonus until the start of the next round.

cards.name: Bad im Drachenblut

abilities.name: Unverwundbar
abilities.text: Attacks against you deal -1 damage,to a minimum of 0, for each die in the attack roll that is a 6.

cards.flavor: Subject of many songs and tales, Siegfried's legend grew with every telling from the warrior-hero that he was to a near -invulnerable dragons-slayer who was able to hide in plain view, and who wielded the sword of Odin himself.
cards.raw_source_text: Unverwundbar: Attacks against you deal -1 damage,to a minimum of 0, for each die in the attack roll that is a 6.

cards.name: Theoderich der Grosse

abilities.name: Nächtlicher Angriff
abilities.text: Once per round, after an attack misses you, you may immediately make an attack, if possible. If you do, you get -1 speed on your next turn. This attack deals +1 damage.

cards.flavor: An exceptionally talented and trained military commander, Theoderich furthered his conquests with both martial force and aggressive diplomacy, and became the ruler of the Ostrogoths, conqueror of Italy, and regent of the Visigoths.
cards.background:
Ruler of the Ostrogoths, conquerer of Italy, and regent of the Visigoths, Theoderic was a talented and trained military commander who furthered his conquests with both martial force and aggressive diplomacy. His willingness to wrk with Emperor Zeno in Constantinople brought the Italian peninsula under his rule after a swift campaign agaisnt Odoacer, the king of Italy. Always watchful for incursions, he beat back repeated assaults by the Franks, threatened the Vandals into submission, and both appeased and scared the Roman Empire into lasting peace.

Theoderic brings both skilled command and warrior prowess to the anachronism arena. He is nver without both plan and recourse, always prepared to meet any enemy and any situation. Use Theoderic, King of the People, to outmauever and outsmart even the most cunning of enemies.
cards.raw_source_text: Nächtlicher Angriff: Once per round, after an attack misses you, you may immediately make an attack, if possible. If you do, you get -1 speed on your next turn. This attack deals +1 damage.

cards.name: Boethius

abilities.name: Schutzbefohlene
abilities.text: At the start of each round, if no opponent reveals a card with greater initiative than this card, gain +1 speed for this round.

cards.flavor: Magister Officiorum for Theoderich's quasi-Roman regime, Boethius was an expert philosopher, teacher, and translator who always performed his duties to the highest standard.
cards.raw_source_text: Schutzbefohlene: At the start of each round, if no opponent reveals a card with greater initiative than this card, gain +1 speed for this round.

cards.name: Kriegsaxt

abilities.name: Schwertbrecher
abilities.text: If the defender has a sword, you may attack them with this weapon twice each turn, and these attacks deal +2 damage.

cards.flavor: The Germanic commanders utilized their own weaponry and strategies along with the tactics taught to them by the Roman commanders.
cards.raw_source_text: Schwertbrecher: If the defender has a sword, you may attack them with this weapon twice each turn, and these attacks deal +2 damage.

cards.name: Lorica Hamata

abilities.name: Anpassungsfähig
abilities.text: Opponents' attack rolls against you cannot gain bonuses larger than +3.

cards.flavor: These ringed shirts provided excellent defense against slashing cuts and were also effective against thrusts, whilst remaining very flexible. As there were only interlinking rings to give it form, the armor suffered little from wear and could be repaired even when badly damaged.
cards.raw_source_text: Anpassungsfähig: Opponents' attack rolls against you cannot gain bonuses larger than +3.

cards.name: Magister Militum

abilities.name: Heldenanerkennung
abilities.text: If any player has made a critical hit this game, all of your rolls gain +1 for the rest of the game.

cards.flavor: Proclaimed Magister Militum, Theoderich was the real power behind the puppet emperors in Rome for nearly three decades.
cards.raw_source_text: Heldenanerkennung - Reveal: If any player has made a critical hit this game, all of your rolls gain +1 for the rest of the game.

cards.name: Makataimeshekiakiak (Black Hawk)

abilities.name: Wakĥáŋyaŋ Naíč'ižiŋ
abilities.text: Critical hits against you deal normal damage. Your weapon attacks against warriors with one-handed weapons deal +1 damage.

cards.flavor: After fighting against the United States in the war of 1812, Black Hawk's tribal land was sold and his people were forced to leave Saukenok, their home of more than 150 years.
cards.background: N/A Promo Warrior
cards.raw_source_text: Wakĥáŋyaŋ Naíč'ižiŋ: Critical hits against you deal normal damage.  Your weapon attacks against warriors with one-handed weapons deal +1 damage.

cards.name: Íŋyaŋ

abilities.name: Íŋyaŋ Ihángyephičašni
abilities.text: If you are an earth,metal, or wood warrior, and you are hit by a critical hit the attack does not deal damage until the end of your next turn. you gain +2 speed on that turn.

cards.flavor: Overlooking the Black Hills, the sacred mountain Inyan Kara is the dwelling of the Great Spirit and resting place of braves on their way to the hunting ground.
cards.raw_source_text: Íŋyaŋ Ihángyephičašni: If you are an earth,metal, or wood warrior, and you are hit by a critical hit the attack does not deal damage until the end of your next turn. you gain +2 speed on that turn.

cards.name: Íŋyaŋ Wahúkeza

abilities.name: Paĥlógya Čhapĥá
abilities.text: After an opponant uses an action ability, you may make an attack with this weapon , if possible.

cards.flavor: Chased and outnumbered almost 10 to 1, Makataimeshekiakiak's warriors eluded the United States army led by General Winfield Scott for months while attempting to reclaim their lands in Illinois.
cards.raw_source_text: Paĥlógya Čhapĥá: After an opponant uses an action ability, you may make an attack with this weapon , if possible.

cards.name: Wakté Waháčhaŋka

abilities.name: Blihíč'iya
abilities.text: Once per round, when you hit with a basic attack, you may have it deal 0 damage .If you do, all of your rolls this game gain +1.

cards.flavor: With circles of feathers to represent the transition from one world to the next, these shields were functional for warfare and steeped in spiritual tradition.
cards.raw_source_text: Blihíč'iya: Once per round, when you hit with a basic attack, you may have it deal 0 damage .If you do, all of your rolls this game gain +1.

cards.name: Okíčhize Čhaŋnúŋpa

abilities.name: Okíčhize un Yuwínyeyapi
abilities.text: Your support cards gain +4 initiative. at the start of each round, if you won initiative, your attack rolls this round gain +1.

cards.flavor: In addition to being used in a variety of ceremonies, a war chief handing the war pipe to the tribe's medicine man signified the start of war.
cards.raw_source_text: Okíčhize un Yuwínyeyapi: Your support cards gain +4 initiative. at the start of each round, if you won initiative, your attack rolls this round gain +1.

cards.name: Maĥpíya Lúta (Red Cloud)

abilities.name: Okhítĥaŋiŋ
abilities.text: After you are hit by a weapon, your first attack of your next turn gains a damage bonus equal to the attacker's weapon's base damage.

cards.flavor: Red Cloud orchestrated the most successful war against the United States ever fought by a Native American nation, forcing the signing of the Fort Laramie Treaty, which granted lands and rights to the Lakota people along the Bozeman Trail and into the Black Hills.
cards.background:
As a warrior and a statesman, Mahpiya Luta's success in confrontations with the United States Government marked him as one of the most important Lakota leaders of the nineteenth century. He orchestrated the most successful war against the United States ever fought by a Native American nation, forcing the signing of the Fort Laramie Treaty, which granted lands and rights to the Lakota people along the Bozeman Trail and into the Black Hills. His careful stratagems and well-planned attacks led to the destruction of several units of troops outside Fort Kearney, and his keen sense of diplomacy led to both the signing of the peace treaty and to his traveling to Washington, D.C. to advise President Grant.

Mahpiya Luta brings ancient traditions coupled with leadership and initiative to the Anachronism arena. His keen foresight and understanding are invaluable against his enemies. Use Mahpiya Luta, known as Red Cloud, to bring terror to your enemies and send them cowering back to their homes.
cards.raw_source_text: Okhítĥaŋiŋ: After you are hit by a weapon, your first attack of your next turn gains a damage bonus equal to the attacker's weapon's base damage.

cards.name: Wí

abilities.name: Waníkiya
abilities.text: If this is the first card you reveal this game, the initiative of your support cards and all your rolls gain +2. After an opponent rolls doubles, discard this card.

cards.flavor: Above all realms, above the world, Wí smiles on bravery and honesty, protecting respectful braves in battle.
cards.raw_source_text: Waníkiya - Reveal: If this is the first card you reveal this game, the initiative of your support cards and all your rolls gain +2. After an opponent rolls doubles, discard this card.

cards.name: Čhehúpahu Čhaŋksá

abilities.name: Wéeyala s'e Yaĥtáke
abilities.text: Your basic attack rolls gain +2.

cards.flavor: Learning to fight against the Pawnee, Crow, Utes, and Shoshones, Red Cloud turned his experience against Lieutenant Colonel William Fetterman, crushing his troops and forcing the United States government to reconsider their Wyoming and Montana expansion plans.
cards.raw_source_text: Wéeyala s'e Yaĥtáke: Your basic attack rolls gain +2.

cards.name: Napé Lúta Waháčhaŋka

abilities.name: Blihélya Naíč'ižiŋ
abilities.text: If you have less experience than the attacker's initiative for this round, your defense rolls gain +2.

cards.flavor: Reserved for those who had successfully provided for their people, in peacetime the markings represented wisdom, responsibility and resourcefulness, while at war they mean strength, courage and cunning.
cards.raw_source_text: Blihélya Naíč'ižiŋ: If you have less experience than the attacker's initiative for this round, your defense rolls gain +2.

cards.name: Itĥáŋčhaŋ Tĥawápĥaha

abilities.name: Wóohitike
abilities.text: Once per turn, when you make an attack or defense roll, you may deal 1 damage to your warrior to re-roll one or both dice. The re-roll gains +2.

cards.flavor: Only those few who had proven themselves time and again in both war and peace could wear the Chieftain's Headdress.
cards.raw_source_text: Wóohitike: Once per turn, when you make an attack or defense roll, you may deal 1 damage to your warrior to re-roll one or both dice. The re-roll gains +2.

cards.name: Tĥašúŋka Witkó (Crazy Horse)

abilities.name: Tókhel Ĥ'aŋphíča Šni
abilities.text: While you have 3 or less life, all of your rolls gain +2.

cards.flavor: Crazy Horse led the counter-attack that demolished Gerge Custer's cavalry regiment during the Battle of the Little Bighorn, leaving not a single man alive.
cards.background:
Leader of his people, courageous warrior, fearless defender of tradition and faith, the war leader Thasunka Witko fought for years against the encroaching United States Army. He led his band of warriors in an assault on Fort Kearney, in the Fetterman Massacre, destroying Lt. Col. William J. Fetterman's forces through superior tactics and courageous fighting. Joining forces with Thanaka Iyotake Thausnka Witko began to stage attacks against the troops escorting th eNorthern Pacific Railroad crews. This culminated with the Battle of Little Bighorn, in which his counterattack demolished George Custer's calvary [sic] regiment, leaving not a single man alive.

Thasunka Witko bring spirited leadership and the belief in certain victory to the Anachronism arena His wild, courageous abandon in battle is frightening to behold and sure to rout even the staunches defenders. Use Thasunka Witko, known as Crazy Horse, to overcom esuperior forces, outmaneuver your enemies, and incessantly pursue every foe.
cards.raw_source_text: Tókhel Ĥ'aŋphíča Šni: While you have 3 or less life, all of your rolls gain +2.

cards.name: Wakíŋyaŋ Šúŋkawakĥaŋ

abilities.name: Theĥíya Okhíčamna
abilities.text: When you make a roll, it gains +2 if the dice are consecutive numbers and you are a Fire, Water, or Wind warrior.

cards.flavor: To walk the path of giving over the path of gaining.
cards.raw_source_text: Theĥíya Okhíčamna: When you make a roll, it gains +2 if the dice are consecutive numbers and you are a Fire, Water, or Wind warrior.

cards.name: Itázipa Blaská

abilities.name: Wakĥáŋ Wičhóĥaŋ s'e Natáŋpi
abilities.text: If the next card you reveal is an Inspiration, you gain +1 speed for that round.

cards.flavor: Used to hunt buffalo, the sinew-backed ash bow was shot from short range while mounted, often within the very midst of the buffalo herd itself.
cards.raw_source_text: Wakĥáŋ Wičhóĥaŋ s'e Natáŋpi - Reveal: If the next card you reveal is an Inspiration, you gain +1 speed for that round.

cards.name: Wakíŋyaŋ Waháčhaŋka

abilities.name: Okíčhize Iyótiyekiya
abilities.text: If you are hit, you may reduce the damage to 1. If you do, you get -1 speed on your next turn.

cards.flavor: Much more than just protection, a warrior's shield held great significance in ceremony. Even after guns were in widespread use, the Lakota warriors brought their richly decorated shields to battle.
cards.raw_source_text: Okíčhize Iyótiyekiya: If you are hit, you may reduce the damage to 1. If you do, you get -1 speed on your next turn.

cards.name: Heyókĥa

abilities.name: Tĥawát'elya
abilities.text: Once each round, after an opponent rolls consecutive numbers, deal 1 damage to that warrior.

cards.flavor: Dedicated to the Thunder Beings, the Thunder Cult epitomized the power of spirituality in warfare.
cards.raw_source_text: Tĥawát'elya: Once each round, after an opponent rolls consecutive numbers, deal 1 damage to that warrior.

cards.name: Tĥatĥaŋka Íyotake (Sitting Bull)

abilities.name: Húŋkešni
abilities.text: Your attack rolls made during the last action of your turn gain +2.
