cards.flavor: With unmatched courage, Sitting Bull defended the lands and traditions of the Lakota people for decades.
cards.background:
Shaman and Chief of the Hunkpapa Sioux and eventually the Lakota nation, Thathanka Iyotake led his warriors on raids against both prospectors and settlers violating the Fort Laramie Treaty in the Black Hills. The United States Army responded, and Thathanka Iyoatake brough together thousands of Lakota, Cheyenne, and Arapaho warriors to fight against these troops. At the Battle of Little Bighorn, these warriors massacred the U.S. 7th Cavalry regiment, led by George Custer, delivering to the United States its most devastating defeat ever at the hands of any Native American tribe.

Thathanka Iyotake brings clairvoyance and determination to the Anachronsim arena. With a clear view of life and liberty, he thinks only of the well-being of his people and will stop at nothing to ensure it. Use Thathanka Iyotake, known as Sitting Bull, to see every situation for what it is and to outlast any enemy with determination and resolve.
cards.raw_source_text: Húŋkešni: Your attack rolls made during the last action of your turn gain +2.

cards.name: Wakĥáŋ Tĥáŋka

abilities.name: Yuwíŋyeyapi
abilities.text: This card's initiative value cannot be changed.

abilities.name: Natáŋ Hiŋglápi
abilities.text: Move your warrior up to one space. Turn this card face down and swap it with another of your face-down cards. Turn the other card face up.

cards.flavor: Sixteen in one, and one in sixteen, the Great Spirit was never born and can never die.
cards.raw_source_text:
Yuwíŋyeyapi: This card's initiative value cannot be changed.
Natáŋ Hiŋglápi - Reveal: Move your warrior up to one space. Turn this card face down and swap it with another of your face-down cards. Turn the other card face up.

cards.name: Waŋhí Čhaŋpí

abilities.name: Opĥé Okhíčamna
abilities.text: After you hit with this weapon, if you do not have an Armor in play, the defender's attack rolls on their next turn cannot be increased.

cards.flavor: Both a thrown and hand weapon, the tomahawk was a general purpose tool, used by braves for any number of tasks both in and out of battle.
cards.raw_source_text: Opĥé Okhíčamna: After you hit with this weapon, if you do not have an Armor in play, the defender's attack rolls on their next turn cannot be increased.

cards.name: Wanáği Wačhípi Waháčhaŋka

abilities.name: Naĥmála naíč'ižiŋ
abilities.text: Your defense rolls gain +3 against attacks that deal more than their base damage.

cards.flavor: The Ghost Dance spread from the Paiute tribe through many reservations before coming to the Sioux, who developed a militaristic approach to the ceremony, adding in weapons, shields, and "ghost shirt" armor.
cards.raw_source_text: Naĥmála naíč'ižiŋ: Your defense rolls gain +3 against attacks that deal more than their base damage.

cards.name: Wanáği Wačhípi

abilities.name: Khinážiŋpi
abilities.text: If you are adjacent to a warrior, move to another space adjacent to that warrior. Your next attack roll this turn gains +2.

cards.flavor: "When you get home you must make a dance to continue five days. Dance four successive nights, and the last night keep up the dance until the morning of the fifth day, when all must bathe in the river and then disperse to their homes." -Wovoka, spiritual leader of the Ghost Dance
cards.raw_source_text: Khinážiŋpi - Action:  If you are adjacent to a warrior, move to another space adjacent to that warrior. Your next attack roll this turn gains +2.

cards.name: Tecumtha (Tecumseh)

abilities.name: Naíč'ižiŋ
abilities.text: Once per round, before you make a defense roll, you may choose to reduce the roll by 2. If you do and the attack misses, gain 2 life.

cards.flavor: A strong leader and a bold adversary, Tecumtha formed a great alliance of Upper-Midwest Native American tribes to fight with the British against the Americans in the war of 1812, where he played an integral part of the capture of Detroit.
cards.background:
A strong leader and a bold adversary, Tecumtha formed a great alliance of Upper-Midwest Native American tribes. A highly respected warrior, even his most bitter enemies admired his uncommon genius and revolutionary thinking. Joining with the British against the Americans in the War of 1812, Tecumtha was an integral part of the capture of Detroit. Forced to abandon Detroit when faced with overwhelming odds, Tecumtha and his force slowed the pursuing American forces to allow the British to safely reach Canada. To this day, Tecumtha is celebrated as a Canadian national hero.

Tecumtha brings selflesss bravery and camaraderie to the Anachronism arena. Valiantly facing even the most desperate battle, he uses every resource at his command, refusing to surrender no matter the odds. Use this leader of the Shawnee people to rally your forces and stop your enemy in their tracks!
cards.raw_source_text: Naíč'ižiŋ: Once per round, before you make a defense roll, you may choose to reduce the roll by 2. If you do and the attack misses, gain 2 life.

cards.name: Haŋbléčheyapi

abilities.name: Waáyataya Waŋyáŋke
abilities.text: After any player rolls doubles, gain +1 speed on your next turn.

cards.flavor: Besides the young, who vision quest at the beginning of the path to adulthood, a person might choose to call for a vision in order to ask a favor from the Great Spirit, or as a thank you for gifts received, or simply to realize a closeness with the Great Spirit.
cards.raw_source_text: Waáyataya Waŋyáŋke: After any player rolls doubles, gain +1 speed on your next turn.

cards.name: Íŋyaŋmila

abilities.name: Zuyá Wičháša Blihéča
abilities.text: Immediately before you attack with this weapon, you may move up to one space.

cards.flavor: Early knives were made from flint or other rock, painstakingly chipped or ground to an edge, and bound to a handle using strips of curved hide.
cards.raw_source_text: Zuyá Wičháša Blihéča: Immediately before you attack with this weapon, you may move up to one space.

cards.name: Mağážu Tĥokáheya Waháčhaŋka

abilities.name: Iwáyuphiya Naíč'ižiŋ
abilities.text: At the end of the round, if you are a Water warrior and in an opponent's attack grid, move up to two spaces.

cards.flavor: Always grateful for the arrival of spring, many Native Americans considered first soaking rain to be a great cleanser of the earth, foretold by the behavior of birds, the spiritual patrollers of the skies.
cards.raw_source_text: Iwáyuphiya Naíč'ižiŋ: At the end of the round, if you are a Water warrior and in an opponent's attack grid, move up to two spaces.

cards.name: Wóphiye Wakĥáŋ

abilities.name: Wótĥawe
abilities.text: Roll one die and place it on this card. Once each turn, you may swap one of the dice in one of your rolls with the die on this card. At the end of the round, increase the die on this card by 1 if possible.

cards.flavor: "We are determined to defend our lands and, if it be the Great Spirit's will, we wish to leave our bones upon them." -Tecumtha, Chief of the Shadows
cards.raw_source_text: Wótĥawe - Reveal: Roll one die and place it on this card. Once each turn, you may swap one of the dice in one of your rolls with the die on this card. At the end of the round, increase the die on this card by 1 if possible.

cards.name: Calum III

abilities.name: Diongbhaltas
abilities.text: Move to any space in any of your attack grids.

cards.flavor: Returning to Scotland at the head of an English army to defeat and kill both Macbeth I and Lulach I, Calum reclaimed his throne and allied himself with the English until William the Conqueror became king, whereupon he mercilessly attacked England.
cards.background:
Forced to seek refuge in England after his father fell in battle, Calum III eventually returned at the head of an English army to defeat and kill both Macbeth I and Lulach I. Reclaiming his birthright to the Scotish throne, he allied himself with the English until William the Conquer become king, whereupon Calum reneged on his agreement and attacked England. A valient warrior king devoted to his country, Calum repeatedly marched against the English, even after they captured and held his son hostage.

Calum III brings glory and a fierce tradition to the anachronism arena. He fought his way through foreign conquest and occupation to free his kingdom, thus ensuring his place in history. Use Calum III, warrior king of the Scots, to smite the forces of your enemies and adversaries.
cards.raw_source_text: Diongbhaltas - Action: Move to any space in any of your attack grids.

cards.name: Naomh Màireid

abilities.name: Fìor-Choisrigte
abilities.text: After you roll doubles on a non-attack roll, your next attack roll is considered to be doubles.

cards.flavor: Coming to the Scottish court as a young lass, this English princess became the wife of Calum III. In her years as queen, she reformed both the court and the church, succeeding where many before her had failed.
cards.raw_source_text: Fìor-Choisrigte: After you roll doubles on a non-attack roll, your next attack roll is considered to be doubles.

cards.name: Claidheamh-Catha dà-Làimhe

abilities.name: Ionnsaigh-Sparraidh
abilities.text: After you hit with this weapon, roll two dice. If the total is less than your experience, move the defender to any space in this weapon's attack grid.

cards.flavor: Fighting in the front ranks with his large two-handed battle sword, Calum III inspired his troops with his bravery and valor.
cards.raw_source_text: Ionnsaigh-Sparraidh: After you hit with this weapon, roll two dice. If the total is less than your experience, move the defender to any space in this weapon's attack grid.

cards.name: Ceannasg Máilleach

abilities.name: Air Fhaiceall ro Chunnart
abilities.text: After you are hit, if the defense roll plus two is greater than the attack roll, the attack deals -1 damage, to a minimum of 0.

cards.flavor: Covering head, neck, and shoulders, a mail coif could protect its wearer from disfigurement as well as death.
cards.raw_source_text: Air Fhaiceall ro Chunnart: After you are hit, if the defense roll plus two is greater than the attack roll, the attack deals -1 damage, to a minimum of 0.

cards.name: Cùmhnant Briste

abilities.name: Brathadh
abilities.text: At the start of any round, you may discard this card. If you do, all opponents' rolls this round get -1 and you may discard an opponent's item.

cards.flavor: Calum was determined to extend his kingdom southwards and take advantage of the upheaval caused by the Norman conquest of England. Making the excuse that he was supporting the claim to the English throne of his brother-in-law Edgar Atheling, Calum broke his treaties with England and invaded five times.
cards.raw_source_text: Brathadh: At the start of any round, you may discard this card. If you do, all opponents' rolls this round get -1 and you may discard an opponent's item.

cards.name: Rob Ruadh MacGriogair

abilities.name: Fear-Claidheimh
abilities.text: Your attack rolls with swords gain +1. Your attack and defense rolls gain +1 against an opponent with a sword.

cards.flavor: Soldier, cattle-rustler, thief, and revolutionary, Rob Roy fought in the Jacobite uprising, emerging with a price on his head for treason; but he could not be brought to justice for many years, always eluding capture and finding new ways to liberate belongings and wealth from the rich.
cards.background:
Byturns a soldier, cattle rustler, theif, and revolutionary, Rob ROy fought against the Dike of Montrose through a sustained campaign of cattle rustling, theft and banditry. He took part in the Jacobite uprising, emerging with a price on his head for treason; but he could not be brought to justince for many years, always eluding capture and finding new ways to liberate belongings and wealth from the Montrose. Instrumental in both the Battle of Sheriffmuir and the Battle of Geln Shiel, Rob Roy used the subtle support of the Duke of Argyll to evade imprisonment and continue his persecution of the Duke of Montrose.

Rob Roy McGregor brings stealth and clandestine warfare tot he anachronism arena. He fought agaisnt the oppresive powers of his time and emerged a hero, adored by the common folk and despised by those he called his enemies. Use Rob ROy, the Scottish Robin Hood, to disrupt your enemies' plans and walk away with victory - and everything else they own!
cards.raw_source_text: Fear-Claidheimh: Your attack rolls with swords gain +1. Your attack and defense rolls gain +1 against an opponent with a sword.

cards.name: Ar-a-mach Cràbhach

abilities.name: Aon Fhìor-Chreideamh
abilities.text: Warriors without Inspirations in play cannot gain bonuses to their defense rolls.

cards.flavor: Dedicated to the restoration of the Stuart kings to the thrones of England and Scotland, the Jacobite Uprisings were fought primarily in Scotland and Ireland.
cards.raw_source_text: Aon Fhìor-Chreideamh: Warriors without Inspirations in play cannot gain bonuses to their defense rolls.

cards.name: Claidheamh Leathann

abilities.name: Marbhaiche Each
abilities.text: While the defender has a cavalry card, this weapon deals +1 damage.

cards.flavor: Requiring intense physical training and conditioning to wield, these heavy, one-handed swords were lethal against both infantry and cavalry.
cards.raw_source_text: Marbhaiche Each: While the defender has a cavalry card, this weapon deals +1 damage.

cards.name: Targaid Gháidhealach

abilities.name: Crùb Fodha
abilities.text: Your defense rolls gain +2 against two-handed weapon attacks.

cards.flavor: In Jacobite times, the Highlander's main means of defense was his targe. These shields were covered in tough hide and deerskin, the front being additionally reinforced and decorated with brass studs and plates.
cards.raw_source_text: Crùb Fodha: Your defense rolls gain +2 against two-handed weapon attacks.

cards.name: Bonaid Trì-Iteagan

abilities.name: Moit na Rèisimeid
abilities.text: Whenever you would roll two dice this round, roll three dice instead and choose which two to use.

cards.flavor: The three-feathered bonnet, along with the white rose of York, was the emblem of the Jacobite sympathizers.
cards.raw_source_text: Moit na Rèisimeid - Reveal: Whenever you would roll two dice this round, roll three dice instead and choose which two to use.

cards.name: Raibeart Brus

abilities.name: A' Feitheamh Ri Fàth
abilities.text: Once per round, your attack rolls with axes this round gain +1 for each of your face-up support cards. Your next axe attack this turn deals +1 damage.

cards.flavor: Known as "the Good King Robert", and one of the most dogged and passionate freedom fighters in history, Robert the Bruce decisively secured Scottish independence from the English at the Battle of Bannockburn, despite overwhelming odds.
cards.background:
Fighting through neumerous setbeacks, Raibeart Brus secured his throne through murder and warfare, nonetheless becoming known as "the good king Raibeart." Considered one of the greatest leaders of any age, he decisively secured Scottish independence from the English at the Battle of Bannokburn, even though outnumbered nearly three to one. After freeing Scotland from English rule, Raibeart attacked, leading his forces on a campaign through northern England, which ultimately forced King Edward II of England to desperatly beg for peace.

Raibeart Brus brings determination and a desire to win at any cost to the anachronism arena. His dogged refusal to yeild and passionate desire for freedom led him to rid Scotland of this tyranny ruling over it. Use Raibeart Brus, the great king of the scots, to wear your opponent into submission, through determination and unwavering vision!
cards.raw_source_text: A' Feitheamh Ri Fàth - Action: Once per round, your attack rolls with axes this round gain +1 for each of your face-up support cards. Your next axe attack this turn deals +1 damage.

cards.name: Damhan-Allaidh Leantalach

abilities.name: Meanbh-Charaid
abilities.text: If you are a Metal, Wind, or Wood warrior, your first attack roll of each round gains +2.

cards.flavor: After suffering defeat in battle, Robert the Bruce was forced to take refuge in a cave. While hiding there, he watched a spider building a web in the cave's entrance. The threads were ripped asunder time after time by the wind, but the spider never gave up and finally succeeded in completing the web. Inspired, Robert again sought battle, this time at Bannockburn.
cards.raw_source_text: Meanbh-Charaid: If you are a Metal, Wind, or Wood warrior, your first attack roll of each round gains +2.

cards.name: Tuagh-Chatha Albannach

abilities.name: Innleachd Chunnartach
abilities.text: After an opponent re-rolls, swaps, increases or decreases the value of any number of dice, your next attack with this weapon deals +1 damage.

cards.flavor: Favoring the battle axe above all other weapons, Robert the Bruce defeated the English knight Henry de Bohun at the start of the Battle of Bannockburn.
cards.raw_source_text: Innleachd Chunnartach: After an opponent re-rolls, swaps, increases or decreases the value of any number of dice, your next attack with this weapon deals +1 damage.

cards.name: Clogaid Phembridge

abilities.name: Do-chrathte
abilities.text: Once per round, when you are attacked, replace your opponent's modifier from their attack grid with any modifier from any of their attack grids.

cards.flavor: Named for Sir Richard Pembridge, these full-face helms provided excellent protection to the head and neck, though they limited both visibility and mobility.
cards.raw_source_text: Do-chrathte: Once per round, when you are attacked, replace your opponent's modifier from their attack grid with any modifier from any of their attack grids.

cards.name: Crùn na h-alba

abilities.name: Tagair Ùghdaras
abilities.text: If you have no Armor in play, reduce all bonuses to your opponent's rolls this round to 0. You may use another head card.

cards.flavor: Won through battle and murder, the crown of Scotland represented much more than kingship: it represented freedom gained at terrible cost, the defiance of tyranny, and the reclamation of a nation.
cards.raw_source_text: Tagair Ùghdaras - Action: If you have no Armor in play, reduce all bonuses to your opponent's rolls this round to 0. You may use another head card.

cards.name: Sgàthach an Eilein Sgiathanaich

abilities.name: Ban-Fhàidh
abilities.text: If your attack roll matches any players' initiative for any round, that attack deals +1 damage.

cards.flavor: Warrior queen and master of a martial school on the Isle of Skye, Queen Sgàthach was a feared and unparalleled combatant. She was possibly the greatest warrior of her time, said never to have been bested by a man in single combat.
cards.background:
Warrior queen and master of martial school on the Isle of Skye, Queen Scathach was a feared and unparalleled combatent. Scathach, along with her daughter Uathach, instructed the youths of Scotland and Ireland in the ways of martial combat and of feats of strength, dexterity, and cunning. Many great heroes sought her priceless teachings, despite the difficulty and dangers involved. She was possibly the greatest warrior of her time and culture, said to have never been bested by a man in single combat.

Queen Scathach of Skye brings expert training and discipline to the anachronism arena. Her skills in combat are rivaled by only a few in history, and her determination and experience carry her through life, defeating every enemy in her path. USe Scathach, Lady of Shadows, to outfight every enemy, calmy deflect every blow and wait for the perfect opportunity to strike!
cards.raw_source_text: Ban-Fhàidh: If your attack roll matches any players' initiative for any round, that attack deals +1 damage.

cards.name: Iombhas Forasna

abilities.name: Na Bhios Ann
abilities.text: Once per round, after an attack or defense roll, you may increase one of the dice you rolled by 1 and decrease one of the dice your opponent rolled by 1.

cards.flavor: One of the techniques used to achieve "illumination," the Iombhas Forasna was used for poetic inspiration and divination, and a Druid or a Filidh could be expected to know it after passing through the eighth year of study.
cards.raw_source_text: Na Bhios Ann: Once per round, after an attack or defense roll, you may increase one of the dice you rolled by 1 and decrease one of the dice your opponent rolled by 1.

cards.name: Tuisg na Grèige

abilities.name: Lannan 'nan Cuairteagan
abilities.text: If you are allowed to have more than one weapon this game and are an Aether, Water, or Wind warrior, you may attack with this weapon twice each turn. The second attack roll with this weapon each turn gains +2.

cards.flavor: A skilled swordsman using two swords could apply offensive pressure while still being able to parry attacks made against her.
cards.raw_source_text: Lannan 'nan Cuairteagan: If you are allowed to have more than one weapon this game and are an Aether, Water, or Wind warrior, you may attack with this weapon twice each turn. The second attack roll with this weapon each turn gains +2.

cards.name: Caithbheart-Chuim Leathrach

abilities.name: Gluasadachd
abilities.text: At the start of each round, if you did not win initiative, you may immediately take one action. If you do, you get -1 speed this round.

cards.flavor: Covering a fighter's torso, these light and flat pieces of armor were often covered in intricate designs that made each one a unique piece of art.
cards.raw_source_text: Gluasadachd: At the start of each round, if you did not win initiative, you may immediately take one action. If you do, you get -1 speed this round.

cards.name: Craobh nan Clach

abilities.name: Binn Ciontaich
abilities.text: At the start of each round, if you win initiative, you may rotate an Aether, Fire, or Metal warrior.

cards.flavor: Many an interloper was hanged on the Tree of the Stones: "... To see the good fruit that grows on the Tree of Stones. Long, speckled fruit it is, wind-swayed by its yellow roots, And like men they are with their feet dancing in the void air."
cards.raw_source_text: Binn Ciontaich: At the start of each round, if you win initiative, you may rotate an Aether, Fire, or Metal warrior.

cards.name: Uilleam Uallas

abilities.name: Cridhe na Gaisgeachd
abilities.text: Your first attack each round with a two-handed non-ranged weapon deals +1 damage. You take +1 damage from critical hits.

cards.flavor: Winning an overwhelming victory against vastly superior forces at the Battle of Stirling Bridge, William Wallace forever secured his place as a hero to the Scottish people.
cards.background: N/A Starter Warrior
cards.raw_source_text: Cridhe na Gaisgeachd: Your first attack each round with a two-handed non-ranged weapon deals +1 damage. You take +1 damage from critical hits.

cards.name: Cluaran na h-Alba

abilities.name: Cha Togar M' Fhearg Gun Dìoladh
abilities.text: Once per round, after you are hit by an opponent without an Armor in play, your next attack deals +2 damage.

cards.flavor: It is said that a raiding party of Danes attacking a Scottish castle came by night and took off their shoes to be as quiet as possible. Reaching the castle moat, they jumped in to swim across. To their surprise, the moat held no water but thistles. Their shouts of pain awoke the defenders of the castle, and the Danes fled.
cards.raw_source_text: Cha Togar M' Fhearg Gun Dìoladh: Once per round, after you are hit by an opponent without an Armor in play, your next attack deals +2 damage.

cards.name: Claidheamh-mòr Gàidhealach

abilities.name: Gearradh Bacaidh
abilities.text: Whenever you hit an opponent with this weapon, their defense rolls get -1 for the rest of the game.

cards.flavor: Often wielded from the back of a charging horse, the highland claymore could cut through entire torsos in a single swing.
cards.raw_source_text: Gearradh Bacaidh: Whenever you hit an opponent with this weapon, their defense rolls get -1 for the rest of the game.

cards.name: Caithbheart-Ruighe Leathrach

abilities.name: Basbaireadh Tuiteamach
abilities.text: Once per turn, after you are missed by an attack, you may immediately make a basic attack against an adjacent opponent at +0. You may use another armor.

cards.flavor: Used to protect wrists and forearms during sword fights, vambraces were both simple to create and one of the most important pieces of armor to a Scottish warrior.
cards.raw_source_text: Basbaireadh Tuiteamach: Once per turn, after you are missed by an attack, you may immediately make a basic attack against an adjacent opponent at +0. You may use another armor.

cards.name: Sporan

abilities.name: Brigh a Bharrachd
abilities.text: After you roll a 1 on any of your dice, you may re-roll that die.

cards.flavor: Worn at the front of the kilt in the traditional dress of men of the Scottish Highlands, these leather or fur pouches were used to carry a wide variety of foodstuffs and other useful items.
cards.raw_source_text: Brigh a Bharrachd: After you roll a 1 on any of your dice, you may re-roll that die.

cards.name: Benrig Medb

abilities.name: Slaintiuil
abilities.text: Twice each round, when an opposing player gains life, you gain that life instead.

cards.flavor: Queen of Connaught in the first century BC, Medb invaded Ulster to acquire the magnificent bull of Cooley. By doing this, she would outdo the possessions of her husband, King Ailill, and become head of the household.
cards.background:
In Celtic Ireland, whichever spouse held the majority of possessions could claim leadership of the household. Queen Medb’s belongings were, in every respect, equal to those of her husband, King Ailill, except for Ailill’s magnificent white bull, which had only one superior in all of Ireland. Medb invaded the province of Ulster to gain possession of the Brown Bull of Cooley and, by doing so, rulership over the kingdom.

Driven by a thirst for power, Medb again resumes her quest for the Donn Cuailnge. Blessed by the goddess Anú and with men prepared to risk death for even a favorable word, use Queen Medb and her singular ambition to overcome all those who oppose her in the Anachronism arena.
cards.raw_source_text: Slaintiuil: Twice each round, when an opposing player gains life, you gain that life instead.

cards.name: Anu

abilities.name: An Mhathair-Uachtaran -
abilities.text: Gain 2 life. Choose an opposing warrior to gain 1 life. All of your rolls during your turn this round gain +1.

cards.flavor: Anu, goddess of fertility and abundance, birthed the Tuatha de Dannan, who, in turn, passed into mythology as the gods and goddesses of the Irish Celts.
cards.raw_source_text: An Mhathair-Uachtaran --Reveal: Gain 2 life. Choose an opposing warrior to gain 1 life. All of your rolls during your turn this round gain +1.

cards.name: Slea Bladhmadh

abilities.name: Glechlaiomh
abilities.text: Attacks with this weapon deal +1 damage while you have a fire inspiration in play.

cards.flavor: The spear was most revered of Celtic weapons, with those belonging to queens and kings often being exeptional works of art as well as instruments of war.
cards.raw_source_text: Glechlaiomh: Attacks with this weapon deal +1 damage while you have a fire inspiration in play.

cards.name: Brat Derg

abilities.name: Seasamh Uasal
abilities.text: Attacks agaisnt you cannot gain damage bonuses if the attacker has fewer cards that match their warrior's culture than you have that match yours.

cards.flavor: Medb accentuated her beauty with the richest of colors and fabrics. Never were men reticent in trying to win her favor.
cards.raw_source_text: Seasamh Uasal: Attacks agaisnt you cannot gain damage bonuses if the attacker has fewer cards that match their warrior's culture than you have that match yours.

cards.name: Donn Cuailnge

abilities.name: Sa
abilities.text: If you hit twice in the same turn, you may immediately make a basic attack, if possible. This attack deals +1 damage.

cards.flavor: Rucht, the swineherd of King Ochne of the Connaught Si, fought a bitter rivalry with Friuch, the swineherd of King Bodb, and was eventually transformed into the Donn Cuailnge, the Brown Bull of Cooley.
cards.raw_source_text: Sa: If you hit twice in the same turn, you may immediately make a basic attack, if possible. This attack deals +1 damage.

cards.name: Brian Boru

abilities.name: Dioltas
abilities.text: You gain +1 speed during the first round of the game if you are opposing a Norse warrior.

abilities.name: An Dara Buille -
abilities.text: Once each game, make an attack with one of your weapons, if possible. After the attack, set your experience to 0.

cards.flavor: Brian, as a child, witnessed the death of most of his Dal Cais tribe, including his mother, at the hands of the Norse. His vengeance set him inexorcably on the path towards becoming the first and only true High King of Ireland.
cards.background: N/A Starter Warrior
cards.raw_source_text:
Dioltas: You gain +1 speed during the first round of the game if you are opposing a Norse warrior.
An Dara Buille -- Action: Once each game, make an attack with one of your weapons, if possible. After the attack, set your experience to 0.

cards.name: Ard Ri

abilities.name: Muinteartha Le Cath
abilities.text: Your attack and defense rolls gain +1 against a warrior who has equal or fewer support cards that match their warrior's culture than you have that match yours.

cards.flavor: Hopelessly bound up in a honorable code of warfare and a paradoxial tradition of timely betrayal and constant antagonism, the Celts of Ireland seemed almost inured to the possibility of unity under a high king.
cards.raw_source_text: Muinteartha Le Cath: Your attack and defense rolls gain +1 against a warrior who has equal or fewer support cards that match their warrior's culture than you have that match yours.

cards.name: Cliabh Mor

abilities.name: Luascadh Trom
abilities.text: If you hit with this weapon, the defender's next support card gets -5 initiative.

cards.flavor: The term "cliabh mor" translates literally to "large sword." So great an impression did these weapon make on the English that the Gaelic term was angelicized to "claymore."
cards.raw_source_text: Luascadh Trom: If you hit with this weapon, the defender's next support card gets -5 initiative.

cards.name: Maille na Lub

abilities.name: Sraonadh
abilities.text: When you are attacked by a sword, axe, or knife, the attacker may choose to reduce their attack roll bonuses to 0. If they do not, the attack deals -1 damage to a minimum of 1.

cards.flavor: The Norse invaders of Ireland were so well equipped that only the Irish nobility could afford to arm and armor themselves in a similar fashion.
cards.raw_source_text: Sraonadh: When you are attacked by a sword, axe, or knife, the attacker may choose to reduce their attack roll bonuses to 0. If they do not, the attack deals -1 damage to a minimum of 1.

cards.name: Culain Tairbh

abilities.name: An Staitse a Reiteach -
abilities.text: Place this card in any space in the arena.

abilities.name: Buntaiste Bhaile
abilities.text: The first defense roll made each turn by opposing warriors in or adjacent to this card's space get -2.

cards.flavor: On the beaches of Clontarf on Good Friday, April 23rd, 1014, Norse power in Ireland was shattered completely and forever in one of the bloodiest battles ever fought on Irish soil.
cards.raw_source_text:
An Staitse a Reiteach -- Reveal: Place this card in any space in the arena.
Buntaiste Bhaile: The first defense roll made each turn by opposing warriors in or adjacent to this card's space get -2.

cards.name: Cu Chulainn

abilities.name: Fioch Fola
abilities.text: At the start of each of your turns, if any opponent revealed a support cards that round with initiative equal to or higher than your life, you may move them to any space in your basic attack grid. If you do, you cannot move more than once during that turn.

cards.flavor: "It is a wonderful thing if I am but one day and one night in the world, provided that my fame and deeds live after me." - Cu Chulainn
cards.background:
Cú Chulainn acquired his name after killing the fierce hound of Culainn. He promised to assume the duties of the guard dog and, from that day forth, became the greatest protector of the province of Ulster. When Queen Medb of Connaught invaded to seize the Brown Bull of Cooley, Cú Chulainn held off her army for days by fighting and defeating her champions in single combat.

Cú Chulainn once again seeks glory in the Anachronism arena. Armed with the fearsome Gae Bolg and driven inexorably towards glory or death by the raven-goddess Morrigan, use the fury of Cú Chulainn to win immortal fame against any that opposes you.
cards.raw_source_text: Fioch Fola: At the start of each of your turns, if any opponent revealed a support cards that round with initiative equal to or higher than your life, you may move them to any space in your basic attack grid. If you do, you cannot move more than once during that turn.

cards.name: Mor Rioghain

abilities.name: Plean Cliste
abilities.text: Once each round, after an opposing warrior gains life, gain +1 speed for the rest of the game.

cards.flavor:
"Over their heads shreiks a lean goddess,
Quickly dancing on the points
Of weapons and shields.
She is the anceint phantom - Queen Morrigan."
- Annals of Lienster
cards.raw_source_text: Plean Cliste: Once each round, after an opposing warrior gains life, gain +1 speed for the rest of the game.

cards.name: Gae Bolg

abilities.name: Ionsai
abilities.text: After you hit with this weapon, your opponent may choose to get -3 on their next roll. If they do not, deal 1 damage to that warrior.

cards.flavor: "You were my beloved comrade, my kin and kindred. Never found I one dearer. Sad will be your death." - Cu Chulainn
cards.raw_source_text: Ionsai: After you hit with this weapon, your opponent may choose to get -3 on their next roll. If they do not, deal 1 damage to that warrior.

cards.name: Duban

abilities.name: Seasamh Ildanach
abilities.text: If you lost initiative this round, you may re-roll one die of one of your attack rolls and one die of one of your defense rolls this round.

cards.flavor: "There is not in the world a warrior whom I shall not drive off." - Cu Chulainn.
cards.raw_source_text: Seasamh Ildanach: If you lost initiative this round, you may re-roll one die of one of your attack rolls and one die of one of your defense rolls this round.

cards.name: Bas Sheasamhach

abilities.name: Comhartha Misneach -
abilities.text: If you lost the previous game, gain 2 life and +3 experience. Otherwise gain 1 life and +1 experience.

cards.flavor: "...and he went to a pillar-stone that is in the plains, and he put his breast-girdle round it that he might not die seated nor lying down, but that he might die standing up. Then arrived the foe all around him, but they dared not go for him, for they thought he was alive." - Annals of Ulster.
cards.raw_source_text: Comhartha Misneach --Reveal: If you lost the previous game, gain 2 life and +3 experience. Otherwise gain 1 life and +1 experience.

cards.name: Donal McCathaigh

abilities.name: Iomaiocht
abilities.text: At the start of this game, choose an opposing warrior. At the end of this game, for the rest of the match, your warrior gains +1 speed against warriors of the chosen warrior's culture.

abilities.name: Aisghairm
abilities.text: Whenever an opponent reveals a card from a different culture than their warrior, they get -2 experience.

cards.flavor: Fortis, ferox et celer.
cards.background:
Dónal McCarthaigh Mór, a man of enormous size and strength, was the King of Munster and the Prince of Desmond. He was learned and wrote exquisite poetry that spoke of his love and yearning for an unnamed woman, though he was forced to marry another out of political concern. He rose into rebellion against the English on more than one occasion, waging the raiding style of warfare that typified the Irish approach to conflict, and which confounded the organized armies of the English.

Now restored to the Anachronism arena, Dónal again seeks a just path through life. Guided by the Dlí Breithim, the laws of the judge, and fighting an unorthodox style of warfare, use this fearsome and passionate king to defeat your foes.
cards.raw_source_text:
Iomaiocht: At the start of this game, choose an opposing warrior. At the end of this game, for the rest of the match, your warrior gains +1 speed against warriors of the chosen warrior's culture.
Aisghairm: Whenever an opponent reveals a card from a different culture than their warrior, they get -2 experience.

cards.name: Dli Breithim

abilities.name: Breithiunas
abilities.text: After an opponent gains a speed bonus from one of their own card abilities or reveals a support card with an initiative greater than 7, deal 1 damage to that warrior.

cards.flavor: The Brehon code regulated each rank of society, from king to slave. Many English settlers adopted the Brehon laws in preference to their own, until the code was finally and brutally extinguished.
cards.raw_source_text: Breithiunas: After an opponent gains a speed bonus from one of their own card abilities or reveals a support card with an initiative greater than 7, deal 1 damage to that warrior.

cards.name: Slea le Scian le Cuma Duilleog

abilities.name: Creacht a Thaoscadh
abilities.text: After an opposing warrior gains life from one of their card abilities, you may immediatly attack that warrior with this weapon, if possible.

cards.flavor: The leaf-blade spear is one of the most immediately recognizable Celtic weapons.
cards.raw_source_text: Creacht a Thaoscadh: After an opposing warrior gains life from one of their card abilities, you may immediatly attack that warrior with this weapon, if possible.

cards.name: Torc Orga

abilities.name: Or Soilseach
abilities.text: You gain +1 speed during the fourth and fifth rounds. Opposing cards cannot reduce your attack rolls, defense rolls, speed or experience.

cards.flavor: Golden torcs were often worn by Celtic warriors, both for their beauty and for protection to the neck.
cards.raw_source_text: Or Soilseach: You gain +1 speed during the fourth and fifth rounds. Opposing cards cannot reduce your attack rolls, defense rolls, speed or experience.

cards.name: Siuloir na Cosach Croise

abilities.name: Ag Siuil ar Chos Chroise
abilities.text: Your warrior is not affected by opposing support cards in spaces in the arena.

abilities.name: Abhog Mor
abilities.text: Once each round, after you roll doubles, you may move one space.

cards.flavor: With the final collapse of the Irish resistence at the end of the sixteenth century, these elite stilt-walking troops left with many of their lords to become mercenaries for France and Spain.
cards.raw_source_text:
Ag Siuil ar Chos Chroise: Your warrior is not affected by opposing support cards in spaces in the arena.

cards.name: Finn MacCumhaill

abilities.name: Cairde agus Comhghuaillithe
abilities.text: At the start of each round, you may discard one of your face up support cards. If you do, put an ally card in your discard pile into play face up. If that card has a reveal ability, use it immediately. This ability cannot return any one ally to play more than once each game.

cards.flavor: Finn MacCumhaill was the leader of the Fianna, who reputedly defeated early Roman landings on Irish shores so savagely that the Romans never returned in numbers.
cards.background:
A warrior, druid, and poet, Finn MacCumhaill led a band of warriors known as the Fianna in the third century AD. They were guardians of the kings of Tara, and of the coast of Ireland. Proud and ferocious warriors, the Fianna had no equals in the land; even to gain entry to the Fianna was a task beyond most. Finn was the greatest of these and, it was said, that no matter what a person spoke in his praise, Finn was three times better.

With the great wisdom granted him by the Salmon of Knowledge, Finn enters the Anachronism arena with more than just strength in arms. Wielding his sword, the Son of the Waves and brandishing the Storm Shield, use Finn, leader of the Fianna, to drive your opponents back whence they came.
cards.raw_source_text: Cairde agus Comhghuaillithe: At the start of each round, you may discard one of your face up support cards. If you do, put an ally card in your discard pile into play face up. If that card has a reveal ability, use it immediately. This ability cannot return any one ally to play more than once each game.

cards.name: An Bradan Feasa

abilities.name: Eagnaiocht
abilities.text: At the start of each round, each opponent who reveals a card with higher initiative than this card gets -1 to all their rolls that round.

cards.flavor: The first person to taste the Salmon of Knowledge would gain enormous wisdom. Finegas the poet caught the fish, but Finn burnt his thumb on the salmon while cooking it. Putting his thumb in his mouth to cool the burn, Finn gained the salmon's power.
cards.raw_source_text: Eagnaiocht: At the start of each round, each opponent who reveals a card with higher initiative than this card gets -1 to all their rolls that round.

cards.name: Mac an Luin

abilities.name: Tonn
abilities.text: If you have a Water inspiration in play, you may use this weapon to attack any adjacent warrior at +2.

abilities.name: Much
abilities.text: When you attack, if the attack and defense rolls tie, deal 1 damage to the defender.

cards.flavor: The weapons of all great Irish warriors had names of thier own. Mac an Luin, "The Son of the Waves," was no exception.
cards.raw_source_text:
Tonn: If you have a Water inspiration in play, you may use this weapon to attack any adjacent warrior at +2.

cards.name: Sgisth Gailbhinn

abilities.name: Cur I dTaca

abilities.name: Clifeal
abilities.text: Once each match, before you make an attack roll, if this card is in your discard pile, the attack roll gains +2.

cards.flavor: Sgaith Gailbhinn, "Storm Shield," was as much of a weapon in Finn's hands as it was a means of defense.

Cur I dTaca: Once each round, before you make a defense roll, your roll gains +2.
Clifeal: Once each match, before you make an attack roll, if this card is in your discard pile, the attack roll gains +2.

cards.name: Bran Agus Sceolang

abilities.name: Conairt Cogaidh -
abilities.text: Place this card in an unoccupied space in the arena.

abilities.name: Ciap
abilities.text: At the end of each round, you may make one basic attack at +2 agaisnt each opposing warrior in or adjacent to this cards space. This attack deals no more than 1 damage.

cards.flavor: Brann and Sceolang were Finn's most faithful companions - intelligent, fierce, and loyal.
cards.raw_source_text:
Conairt Cogaidh --Reveal: Place this card in an unoccupied space in the arena.
Ciap: At the end of each round, you may make one basic attack at +2 agaisnt each opposing warrior in or adjacent to this cards space. This attack deals no more than 1 damage.

cards.name: Ariki Te Wherowhero

abilities.name: Regu
abilities.text: Once each round, when you hit with a bludgeon and the defender has no Armor in play, the defender's next roll gets -2.

abilities.name: Tika
abilities.text: Your attack rolls with bludgeons gain +1.

cards.flavor: Descended from Wikato and Ngati Maniapoto chiefs, Te Wherowhero established his status as a legendary battle leader before being declared king in the face of increasing British aggression.
cards.background:
Te Wherowhero grew up overshadowed by the glories of his father, a war chief of the Waikato tribe. Nevertheless, he became a legendary war leader in his own right after engaging in battles against the Ngati Toa and Ngati Awa. When British settlers began to encroach on ancestral Maori lands, Te Wherowhero’s uncompromising response earned him respect and prestige. He went on to become the first king of the Maori, and was one of the key figures in the signing of the Treaty of Waitangi, considered the founding document of New Zealand as a nation.

Invested with the power of his ancestors, use Te Wherowhero, king of the Maori, to wage fearless and uncompromising war upon any opponent who faces him in the Anachronism arena.
cards.raw_source_text:
Regu: Once each round, when you hit with a bludgeon and the defender has no Armor in play, the defender's next roll gets -2.
Tika: Your attack rolls with bludgeons gain +1.

cards.name: Treaty of Waitangi

abilities.name: Whakawiri Teka
abilities.text: Choose another warrior. Exchange the experience values of your warrior and the chosen warrior.

cards.flavor: Despite the controversy that still surrounds it, "The Treaty", as it is called by many New Zealanders, is regarded as the founding document of New Zealand as a nation.
cards.raw_source_text: Whakawiri Teka - Reveal: Choose another warrior. Exchange the experience values of your warrior and the chosen warrior.

cards.name: Taiaha

abilities.name: Kuruté
abilities.text: Attacks made with this weapon against adjacent opponents deal -1 damage.

cards.flavor: The wooden, tongue-shaped blade of the taiaha carries significant meaning in a culture where to stick one's tongue  out at an enemy is a sign of defiance and an implicit challenge.
cards.raw_source_text: Kuruté: Attacks made with this weapon against adjacent opponents deal -1 damage.

cards.name: Pa

abilities.name: Whakakaha
abilities.text: Place this card in any unoccupied space in the arena.

abilities.name: Whita
abilities.text: If an opposing warrior ends their turn in this card's space, deal 1 damage to them. While you are in or adjacent to this card's space, attacks against you cannot gain damage bonuses.

cards.flavor: The Maori were able to build these earthen forts almost overnight. By the end of the Maori Wars, the evolution of the pa had made it an almost impregnable defensive structure.

Whita: If an opposing warrior ends their turn in this card's space, deal 1 damage to them. While you are in or adjacent to this card's space, attacks against you cannot gain damage bonuses.

cards.name: Hei-Tiki

abilities.name: Te Tuatahi Tangata
abilities.text: At the start of each round, the player who won initiative that round may choose to go last. If they do not, you may turn one of that player's support cards face-down. Turn that card face-up at the end of the round.

cards.flavor: In Maori myth, Tiki was  the first man. He found the first woman, Marikoriko, in a pond.  Marikoriko seduced him, and from their union was born Hine-Kau-Ataata.
cards.raw_source_text: Te Tuatahi Tangata: At the start of each round, the player who won initiative that round may choose to go last. If they do not, you may turn one of that player's support cards face-down. Turn that card face-up at the end of the round.

cards.name: Hatupatu

abilities.name: Whakahaere
abilities.text: The first time you make a critical hit that hit deals normal damage and you gain 4 life and 6 experience.

cards.flavor: The youngest of four sons, Hatupatu's elder siblings bullied him remorselessly as a child. However, he grew to become a great warrior of the Arawa tribe, slaying an enemy chief, Raumati, from Tauranga.
cards.background:
The youngest of four sons, Hatupatu was cruelly treated by his elder brothers. Despite this, Hatupatu grew up to become a chief of some note. Legend tells of his escape from the bird-woman Kurangaituku, by hiding behind a rock and leading her into a hot spring. Having grown into a man of ability and confidence, he led his people in battle against their enemy, chief Raumati, who had burned the Te Arawa canoe. Killing this powerful enemy, he gained great respect and mana, and set himself on the path to becoming one of the most famous of all Maori warriors.

Cunning and favored by the spirits, use Hatupatu, hero of the Arawa, to dispatch your opponents in the Anachronism arena through trickery and determination.
cards.raw_source_text: Whakahaere:The first time you make a critical hit that hit deals normal damage and you gain 4 life and 6 experience.

cards.name: Tane Mahuta

abilities.name: Kaiwhakangaio
abilities.text: Once each round, after you and an opponent each roll two dice, if you are a Water, Wind, or Wood warrior, you may deal 1 damage to your warrior. If you do, swap your highest die with the opponent's highest die.

cards.flavor: God of trees and birds, he was the first son of Ranga-Nui and Papa-Tu-A-Nuku. Growing tired of the darkness in which he lived due to his parents' close embrace, he forced them apart, creating the sky and the earth.
cards.raw_source_text: Kaiwhakangaio: Once each round, after you and an opponent each roll two dice, if you are a Water, Wind, or Wood warrior, you may deal 1 damage to your warrior. If you do, swap your highest die with the opponent's highest die.

cards.name: Mere

abilities.name: Hunga Ora
abilities.text: After you attack with this weapon, gain +1 speed this round.

abilities.name: Mama
abilities.text: You may use another weapon.

cards.flavor: A flat war club measuring about twelve inches in length, the mere had a round, sharpened top and was often decorated with koru patterns.
cards.raw_source_text:
Hunga Ora: After you attack with this weapon, gain +1 speed this round.
Mama: You may use another weapon.

cards.name: Te Kohatu-O-Hatupatu

abilities.name: Haere Ninihi
abilities.text: Place this card in any space in the arena.

abilities.name: Whakapeke
abilities.text: Once each round, when a warrior in this card's space is dealt damage, they may reduce that damage by 2 to a minimum of 1. After a warrior in this space is critically hit, discard this card.

cards.flavor: Pursued by Karungaituku, a vicious bird-woman, Hatupatu pounded on a large rock, crying, "Te kohatu n ei-e matiti matata." The rock immediately split open allowing Hatupatu to leap inside and hide.
